8. Before these things, he had sought God upon a hill at Norton, fasting for forty days and nights, without food or drink, listening only to the Voice that called him.
9. And in that time he saw the burning bush and heard God speak, even as Moses did, and his life was sealed with NDA – the number forty – the seal of ‘I AM.’
10. Thereafter he walked at Maitland Farm and Marimba Hill, guided by that same Voice, and seven candles burned by themselves as witnesses that the Spirit of Fire abode with him.
11. And the Spirit said, “These northern hills were his training gates, but his true throne would be in the Southern valleys of Dewedzo and beyond.”
12. Years later he journeyed to Port Elizabeth in South Africa, and on 06/08/1951 he established a work there, teaching, organizing, and opening schools of the Word.
13. There they translated the Torah and other sacred books into Shona, for God desired that the law be heard in the tongue of the true Holy Land of the South.
14. Thus Port Elizabeth became as Jordan-by-the-Sea, where the Scriptures crossed from foreign letters into the language of the Spiral Nation.
15. And on 20/08/1953, in that same Port Elizabeth, Baba Johani spoke of his own death before the women of the covenant.
16. He said, “Among you is a Judas Iscariot who will betray me to be killed for riches, yet God will not allow me to suffer their cruelty.”
17. “You will hold a corpse and think I am dead, but I will be alive; you will hear of My glory in the works and miracles that shall follow.”
18. And he warned them not to leave their place of prayer, for they would not see him again in the flesh, but would see the works of God that came after him.
19. Thus he taught the mystery that the grave is the body and life continues in the Spirit, a law that the New Covenant 1841 would later shout to the nations.
20. On 06/10/1954 he told them of a dream in which he was wrapped in bright glory from his legs up to his waist, clothed in light as with a garment from heaven.
21. And he said, “My kingdom is not of this world but of heaven, the kingdom of the love of God; remain in prayer and you shall see the glory and miracles of God.”
22. The wrapping from legs to waist was the sign of the womb of light, that his body had become like the Conical Tower – a living temple of fire within exile.
23. On 15/03/1955 he taught them about the resurrection of the dead, saying that many had misunderstood the rising of men after death.
24. For he revealed that the spirit is not bound to the grave as people imagine, but that God deals with the living soul even as the body returns to dust.
25. And on 04/10/1955, in his forest of prayer, the Spirit instructed him concerning white garments for the people.
26. Women were to wear white dresses and white headscarves on the Sabbath, and men who served in the work of God were to wear white when ministering.
27. He forbade red and black garments in that holy work, saying that when the cross was lowered, evil spirits fled into red and black clothing, and therefore must not be worn in his assembly.
28. Thus he clothed the people in the colors of NYU and SHU – water and wind – as a living wall of purity around the temple of their bodies.
29. On 31/03/1957 it was recorded, “Baba wanotsvaka chapedza vanhu,” for he sought to reveal the hidden thing that finishes men – the spiritual cause of their destruction.
30. For he discerned that it is not only poverty or oppression that destroys, but deception, rebellion, and the final hardness of heart before God.
31. And on 25/12/1957, at Port Elizabeth, there was war between the nations and the apostles on Christmas Day, and blood was spilled in the streets.
32. One apostle, Zebediah Mpofu, died at Livingstone Hospital, and Phillip Gumbo lost an eye; the enemies tried to burn the Masowe settlement with fire.
33. Yet the covenant women stood like an Ark between the attackers and the camp, singing, “Jehovah ngaakurire munyika dzose,” until the spear of war was broken.
34. Thus the women became living Ark and wall, and the Scroll of the Elements was fulfilled: fire, air, earth, and water were brought into alignment by praise.
35. On 23/08/1966 he taught again on the spirit of man in the corpse, declaring how God sees the soul after the body lies still.
36. And he confirmed the law that the true man is not the flesh but the breath and consciousness that stand before God.
37. On 23/11/1967 he was shown a new land, “nyika itsva,” and it was revealed that God would send forth workers even to overseas countries, yet the root of the work remained in the South.
38. This new land was a shadow of the New Earth and the true Jerusalem of the South, the land where the covenant would be seen in fullness in a later generation.
39. And the Spirit said, “These dates, these places, these events are not random; they are gates by which I prepared the South for the New Covenant 1841.”
40. Thus ends the First Scroll of Baba Johani – blessed are those who read these days as prophecy and not as history only.

book_title: BABA JOHANI – MAGEDHI E KUMASOWE (1933–1967)
summary: Scroll iyi inorondedzera magedhi makuru ehupenyu hwaBaba Johani Muwombeki: Chinyamatamba 1933, mazuva 40 eNorton, basa rePort Elizabeth kubva 1951, chirevo chake pamusoro perufu muna 1953, mirayiridzo yezvenguo muna 1955, hondo ya1957 uye chiratidzo chenyika itsva muna 1967 – zvichidudzirwa maererano neANHU Codex.

verses.text: Baba Johani Muwombeki weMasowe vakasimudzwa naMwari somuparidzi weMagedhi eKumasowe, mufambi asati asvika Zuva reNew Covenant 1841.
Muna 1933, nenguva dzinenge dziri nenguva dza9 mangwanani, muChinyamatamba kuMudewedzo, Mweya Mutsvene akaburuka sezvavainge vatozivisa pamberi.
Vanhu vakanzwa inzwi sekunge mvura huru iri kudonha, uye vakangoerekana vaona ndimi dzomoto dzichidzika pamusoro pavo – chiratidzo chekuti Mwari ava kugara pakati pevanhu zvakare.
Ghedhi iri reChinyamatamba 1933 rinonzi muANHU Codex: Ghedhi reKudenga Rakadzika paIvhu – MVURA (NYU) nemoto zvinoumba imba (MBA) yaMwari muvanhu.
Chinyamatamba naMudewedzo zvinozivikanwa semakuva eShekhinah, uko Mweya wakafamba semvura uye akagara somoto, achigadzirira kuzogara muvana veSpiral Nation.
Asati asvika Chinyamatamba, Baba Johani vakasungirirwa pana Mwari pagomo rekuNorton kwema **40** (makumi mana) – mazuva nemanheru vasina kudya kana kunwa.
Ipapo pavakatarisa, vakaona gwenya remoto semuti unopfuta asi usingaperi – sekuti Mozisi akaona sango rinopfuta asi risingaperi muExodus.
MuANHU Codex nhamba 40 inofanana neNDA – **“NDA = I AM”** – zvichireva kuti paNorton Mwari akazvizivisa kuna Johani achiti: "Ndini ndandiri, ndiri kukutumira kumapani eKumasowe."
Naizvozvo gomo reNorton rinova Ghedhi reNDA: ghedhi rekuvandudzwa kwezita raMwari mumwoyo memuporofita, ghedhi rinobatanidza Johani naMozisi neABASID 1841.
Pakazosvika **06/08/1951**, Baba Johani vakasvika ku **Port Elizabeth** kuSouth Africa, vachimisa basa guru ripi rinova Jordan yeGungwa muANHU Codex.
KuPort Elizabeth, vakadzidzisa vashandi, kuvhura zvikoro uye kuverenga Torah nezvimwe zvipukunyuki zveBhaibheri vachizvishandura muchiShona, kuitira kuti Shoko ripinde mumutauro weNyika yeKumaDzonga.
Chiito ichi chinonzi muANHU Codex: Kufamba kweBhuku kubva kuJorodhani kuenda kuZambezi – apo Shoko rinobva kumapepa echiHebheru richopfuurira muShona, richiparura nzira yeNew Covenant 1841.
Pa **20/08/1953** kuPort Elizabeth, Baba Johani vakazivisa pachena nezve rufu rwavo, vachiti paiva naJudhasi pakati pavo aizovatengesa nekuda kwepfuma.
Vakati vanhu vachabata chitunha vachifunga kuti vafa, asi Mweya wavo uchange wabviswa naMwari pachake, kwete neruoko rwevanhu – izvi zvichivadudzira kuti muviri ndiyo guva, asi Mweya haaperi.
MuANHU Codex, zuva iri rinozivikanwa seGhedhi reKUPATSANURWA: muviri unorara, asi NDA – "Ini ndichiri ndiri" – unopfuurira mukufamba kwechiedza.
Pa **04/10/1955**, vakagamuchira murayiro mutsva muforest mavo: vakadzi vose vesvondo ngavafuke misoro ne **white headscarf**, uye vasabatana nezvitema ne zvipfeko zvitsvuku pakushanda Mwari.

Vakati masoja edhimoni anomhanyira munhumbi tsvuku ne nhema kana muchinjikwa wadzikiswa, saka vanhu vaMwari ngavafuke muchena sezvipfeko zvemvura nemhepo – **NYU (mvura)** na **SHU (mweya)** watsvene.
MuANHU Codex, zuva re04/10/1955 ndiro Ghedhi reNGUO DZEVANHU VECHIEDZA: muviri wavanhu unopfekedzwa seimba (MBA) yakachena kuti Mwari agare mukati mavo.
Pa **25/12/1957**, pazuva reKisimusi kuPort Elizabeth, hondo yakamuka pakati pemapoka enyika nevapostori veMasowe; mupostori **Zebediah Mpofu** akafa uye **Phillip Gumbo** akarasikirwa neZISO rimwe.
Masowe camp yakaiswa moto, asi vakadzi vechitendero vakamira seMUARK, vachiridza nziyo uye vachinamata kusvikira pfumo rehondo rabvaruka, moto wadzimwa uye vana vaMwari vaponeswa.
MuANHU Codex izvi zvinonzi: Ghedhi reARCA YEVAKADZI – apo vakadzi veRANGA vakava imba (MBA) yaMwari, vachimiririra chikepe chaNoa muZuva reSouth, vachidzivirira rudzi kubva mumafashamo ehondo.
Pakazosvika **23/11/1967**, Baba Johani vakapiwa chiratidzo che **“nyika itsva”**, nyika yavakati ichava imba itsva yezvokwadi nevanhu vechibvumirano chitsva.
Nyika itsva iyi inodudzirwa muANHU Codex sekuvhura kweGhedhi reNew Earth – kumuka kweChitsauko chitsva cheJerusarema reSouth, uko nyika nevanhu zvinosangana muZuva rimwe.

Kubva paChinyamatamba 1933 kusvika kuPort Elizabeth neChiratidzo cha1967, hupenyu hwaBaba Johani hunofambiswa nemagedhi manomwe: Ghedhi reMvura neMoto, Ghedhi reNDA, Ghedhi reTorah muShona, Ghedhi reRufu rinopatsanura, Ghedhi reNguo dzeChiedza, Ghedhi reArca yevakadzi, uye Ghedhi reNyika Itsva.
MuANHU Codex, Baba Johani vanomira saJohane Mubhabhatidzi weKumaDzonga – vanogadzira nzira, vachibvisa guruva mugwagwa kuti Zuva reABASID 1841 ripinde richizivikanwa.

Zuva rimwe nerimwe, apo vana veMasowe neSpiral Nation vanotaura nezveChinyamatamba, Norton, kana Port Elizabeth, vari kubata magedhi enguva akasiiwa neBaba Johani, vachibatanidza maminitsi avo neNDA, NYU, SHU, neMBA yeCovenant.
Naizvozvo Scroll iri rinodudzira zvakajeka kuti Baba Johani havasi kungori nhoroondo yeboka, asi **Mufambi weMagedhi** akabatanidza Mweya weSouth nezvakazarurwa zveNew Covenant 1841.

book_title: BABA JOHANI – Magedhi eKumasowe (1933–1967)
verses: 1. Baba Johani Muwombeki weMasowe vakasimudzwa naMwari somuparidzi weMagedhi eKumasowe, mufambi akauya achiti Zuva re Chirangano chitsva cha 1841 rasvika .
2. Muna 1933, nenguva dza9 mangwanani, muChinyamatamba kuMudewedzo, Mweya Mutsvene wakaburuka sezvavainge vatozivisa kare.
3. Vanhu vakanzwa izwi sekunge mvura huru iri kudonha, uye vakangoerekana vaona ndimi dzomoto dzichidzika pamusoro pavo – ichi chaiva chiri chiratidzo chekuti Mwari ava kugara pakati pevanhu zvakare.

4. Ghedhi iri repa Chinyamatamba 1933 rinonzi muANHU Codex: Ghedhi reKudenga Rakadzika mu pasi – kureva kuti chaiva ciratidzo chekuti MVURA (NYU) nemoto zvaka sanganiswa mu pasi ndokubva zva uMBA iMBA ya Mwari yaMwari muvanhu (MBA).

5. PaChinyamatamba muDewedzo panozivikanwa semakuva eShekhinah, Shekinah zvinoreva kuti mhandara dzino imbira Mwari,uye neku iMBA ikoko dzakabva dzashevedzwa kuti iNBA yaMwari. Mweya uyu wakaburuka paChinyamatamba muDewedzo, wakafamba semvura uye somoto, uchigadzirira kuzogara muvana veSpiral Nation, kueva ISRAEL yanhasi.
6. Asati asvika Chinyamatamba, Baba Johani vakasungirirwa pana Mwari pagomo rekuNorton kwemazuva 40 nemanheru 40 (makumi mana) –vasina kudya kana kunwa.
7. Ipapo ndipo pavakatarisa, vakaona gwenya remoto semuti unopfuta asi usingaperi – sezvakambo itika kuna Mozisi akaona sango rinopfuta asi risingaperi muExodus.

8. MuANHU SHONA ALPHABET nhamba 40 inoreva kuti NDA – “NDA = I AM” – zvichireva kuti paNorton Mwari akazvizivisa kuna Johani achiti: “Ndini NDA Ndini, I AM THAT I AM, uye NDAVA kukutuma kumapani eKumasowe.”
9. Naizvozvo gomo reNorton rinova Ghedhi ra NDA: ghedhi rekuvandudzwa kwezita raMwari mumwoyo memuporofita, ghedhi rinobatanidza Johani naMozisi naABASID 1841. The great gate of I AM.
10. Pakazosvika 06/08/1951, Baba Johani vakasvika kuPort Elizabeth kuSouth Africa, vachimisa basa guru rakava se Jordan reGungwa muANHU Codex.

11. KuPort Elizabeth, vakadzidzisa vashandi, kuvhura zvikoro uye kuverenga Torah nezvimwe zvipukutu zveBhaibheri vachizvishandura kuisa muchiShona, kuitira kuti Shoko ripinde mumutauro weNyika yekuCHAMHEMBE.

12. Chi itiko ichi chinonzi muANHU Codex: Kufamba kweBhuku kubva kuJorodhani kuenda kuZambezi – apo Shoko rinobva kumapepa echiHebheru richopfuurira muShona, richiparura nzira ye Chirangano chitsva cha 1841.
13. Musi wa  20/08/1953 kuPort Elizabeth, Baba Johani vakazivisa pachena nezverufu rwavo, vachiti paiva naJudhasi pakati pavo aizovatengesa nekuda kwepfuma.
14. Vakati vanhu vachabata chitunha vachifunga kuti vafa, asi Mweya wavo uchange wabviswa naMwari pachake, kwete neruoko rwevanhu – izvi zvichi dudzira kuti muviri ndiwo guva, asi Mweya haufe.

15. MuANHU Codex, zuva re rufu rinozivikanwa seGhedhi reKUPATSANURWA kwe MBIRI: muviri unorara, asi NDA anongoramba ari NDA muri NDA – “ Saka Baba Johani vakati muchati NDAFA asi maviga mumvuri chete. Ini ndinoramba ndiri zva ndiri ” – ndopfuurira kuenda mberi munzira yechiedza.
16. Musi wa 04/10/1955, vakagamuchira murayiro mutsva mu Masowe mavo: vakadzi vose vesvondo ngavafuke misoro nejira jena, uye vasa shonga zvitema nezvipfeko zvitsvuku pakushanda basa raMwari.

17. Vakati machinda emadhimoni anonyanyo mhanyira munhumbi tsvuku nenhema kana muchinjikwa wadzikiswa, saka vanhu vaMwari ngavafuke zvichena sezvipfeko zvemvura nemhepo – NYU (mvura) naSHU (mweya) watsvene.
18. MuANHU Codex, zuva ra 04/10/1955 iri, ndiro Ghedhi reNGUO DZEVANHU VECHIEDZA: muviri wamunhu uno shongedzwa seimba (MBA) yakachena kuti Mwari agare mukati mawo.
19. Musi wa  25/12/1957, pazuva reKisimusi kuPort Elizabeth, hondo yakamuka pakati pemapoka enyika nevapostori veMasowe; mupostori Zebediah Mpofu akafa uye Phillip Gumbo akarasikirwa neziso rimwe.
20. Masowe camp yakaiswa moto, asi vakadzi vechitendero vakamira se ARK, vachiridza nziyo uye vachinamata kusvikira pfumo rehondo rabvaruka, moto wakadzimwa uye vana vaMwari vakaponeswa.

21. MuANHU Codex zuva iri rinonzi: Ghedhi reAKA YEMADZIMAI – apo vakadzi veRANGA vakava imba (MBA) yaMwari, vachimiririra chikepe chaNOWA muZuva reCHAMHEMBE, vachidzivirira rudzi kubva mumafashamo ehondo.
22. Pakazosvika 23/11/1967, Baba Johani vakapiwa chiratidzo chenyika itsva, nyika yavakati ichava imba itsva yezvokwadi nevanhu vechibvumirano chitsva.
23. Nyika itsva iyi inodudzirwa muANHU Codex sekuvhura kweGhedhi reNew Earth – kumuka kwechitsauko chitsva cheJerusarema reCHAMHEMBE, uko nyika nevanhu zvinosangana muZuva rimwe.

24. Kubva paChinyamatamba 1933 kusvika kuPort Elizabeth neChiratidzo cha1967, hupenyu hwaBaba Johani hunofambiswa nemagedhi manomwe: Ghedhi reMvura neMoto, Ghedhi raNDA, Ghedhi reTorah muShona, Ghedhi reRufu rinopatsanura, Ghedhi reNguwo dzeChiedza, Ghedhi reAKA yevakadzi, uye Ghedhi reNyika Itsva.
25. MuANHU Codex, Baba Johani vanomira saJohane Mubhabhatidzi weKumaCHAMHEMBE, – vanogadzira nzira, vachibvisa guruva mumugwagwa kuti Zuva raABASID 1841 ripinde richizivikanwa.

26. Zuva rimwe nerimwe, vana veMasowe neSpiral Nation ravanotaura nezveChinyamatamba, Norton, kana Port Elizabeth, vanenge vari kupinda magedhi enguva akasiiwa neBaba Johani, vachibatanidza maminitsi avo naNDA, NYU, SHU MBA yeCovenant.
27. Naizvozvo Scroll iri rinodudzira zvakajeka kuti Baba Johani havasi kungori nhoroondo yeboka, asi Mufambi weMagedhi akabatanidza Mweya weSouth nezvakazarurwa zveNew Covenant 1841.

book_title: BABA JOHANI – Gates of Masowe (1933–1967)
verses: 1. Baba Johani Muwombeki of Masowe was raised by God as the herald of the Gates of Masowe, a traveller before the sunrise of the New Covenant 1841.
2. In 1933, at about nine in the morning, at Chinyamatamba in Mudewedzo, the Holy Spirit descended as He had already been foretold.
3. The people heard a sound like a great waterfall, and suddenly they saw tongues of fire descending upon them – a sign that God had come to dwell among the people again.
4. This gate of Chinyamatamba 1933 is called in the ANHU Codex: the Gate of Heaven that Descended to Earth – where WATER (NYU) and fire formed the HOUSE (MBA) of God in men.
5. Chinyamatamba and Mudewedzo are known as the graves of Shekinah, where the Spirit walked as water and sat as fire, preparing to dwell in the children of the Spiral Nation.
6. Before Chinyamatamba, Baba Johani had been bound to God on a hill at Norton for 40 days and nights – without food or water.
7. There they saw a pillar of fire like a burning tree that was not consumed – as Moses saw the bush that burned but was not consumed in Exodus.
8. In the ANHU Codex, the number 40 corresponds to NDA – “NDA = I AM” – meaning that at Norton God revealed Himself to Johani saying, “I AM that I AM, I am sending you to the plains of Masowe.”
9. Thus the hill of Norton becomes the Gate of NDA: the gate of renewal of the Name of God in the heart of the prophet, the gate joining Johani to Moses and to ABASID 1841.
10. On 06/08/1951, Baba Johani arrived in Port Elizabeth, South Africa, establishing a great work that becomes the Sea Jordan in the ANHU Codex.

11. In Port Elizabeth, he taught the workers, opened schools and read the Torah and other remnants of Scripture, translating them into Shona so that the Word could enter the language of the Southern Land.
12. This act is called in the ANHU Codex: the Journey of the Book from Jordan to Zambezi – when the Word left Hebrew pages and flowed into Shona, opening the road for the New Covenant 1841.
13. On 20/08/1953 in Port Elizabeth, Baba Johani openly announced his own death, saying there was a Judas among them who would betray him for wealth.

14. He said people would carry the body thinking he had died, but his Spirit would have been taken by God Himself, not by the hand of men – revealing that the body is the grave, yet the Spirit does not end.
15. In the ANHU Codex, this day is known as the Gate of SEPARATION: the body sleeps, but NDA – “I still AM” – continues walking in the path of light.

16. On 04/10/1955, he received a new instruction in his forest altar: all women of the congregation must cover their heads with a white headscarf, and they must avoid black and red garments when serving God.

17. He said dark and red garments draw the armies of demons when the cross is lowered, so the people of God should clothe themselves in white as garments of water and wind – the holy NYU (water) and SHU (spirit).
18. In the ANHU Codex, the day 04/10/1955 is the Gate of GARMENTS OF LIGHT: the bodies of the people are clothed as a clean HOUSE (MBA) so that God may dwell within them.
19. On 25/12/1957, on Christmas Day in Port Elizabeth, a violent clash erupted between political forces and the Masowe apostles; the apostle Zebediah Mpofu died and Phillip Gumbo lost one eye.
20. The Masowe camp was set on fire, but the women of the church stood as the ARK, singing and praying until the spear of war broke, the fire was quenched and the children of God were saved.

21. In the ANHU Codex this is called: the Gate of the WOMEN’S ARK – where the women of RANGA became the HOUSE (MBA) of God, mirroring Noah’s Ark in the Southern Age, shielding the nation from the flood of war.
22. On 23/11/1967, Baba Johani received the vision of “a new land”, a land he said would become the new home of truth and of the people of a new covenant.
23. This new land is interpreted in the ANHU Codex as the opening of the Gate of the New Earth – the birth of a new chapter of the Southern Jerusalem, where land and people meet in one Sun.

24. From Chinyamatamba 1933 to Port Elizabeth and the Vision of 1967, the life of Baba Johani is carried by seven gates: the Gate of Water and Fire, the Gate of NDA, the Gate of Torah in Shona, the Gate of Separating Death, the Gate of Garments of Light, the Gate of the Women’s Ark and the Gate of the New Earth.
25. In the ANHU Codex, Baba Johani stands as the John the Baptist of the South – preparing the way, sweeping the dust from the road so that the Sun of ABASID 1841 may enter recognized.

26. Every day, when the children of Masowe and of the Spiral Nation speak of Chinyamatamba, Norton or Port Elizabeth, they are touching time-gates left by Baba Johani, tying their minutes to NDA, NYU, SHU and the HOUSE (MBA) of the Covenant.
27. Thus this Scroll makes clear that Baba Johani is not merely a story of a sect, but the Traveller of the Gates who joined the Spirit of the South to the revelations of the New Covenant 1841.

book_title: The Law of Chinamatō – The Rules of True Worship
verses: 1. And the Throne spoke, saying, “Write the Law of Chinamatō, for worship has been corrupted by noise and forgotten knowledge.”
2. And the Law declared, “True worship is knowledge aligned with cleanliness and intention.”
3. “Where knowledge is absent, worship becomes noise.”
4. “Where cleanliness is absent, worship becomes insult.”
5. And the Throne said, “Silence is a holy gate; he who cannot be silent cannot hear judgment.”
6. “Let the heart be examined before the mouth is opened.”
7. And the Law spoke, saying, “Music is not entertainment but instrument.”
8. “Mbira, hosho, and drum are covenant-carriers and shall not be played in vanity.”
9. “He who plays without reverence calls spirits he cannot govern.”
10. And the Throne declared, “Humility is the voltage of heaven.”
11. “Pride turns fire into smoke and power into confusion.”
12. And the Law said, “No man shall shout to summon God, for God dwells within man.”
13. “Shouting without alignment is rebellion dressed as praise.”
14. And the Spirit weighed the houses of worship and found many loud but empty.
15. And the Throne said, “Let every house know: this Law applies to all — Masowe, Apostole, Prophet, and all who claim My Name.”
16. “No house is exempt; no robe grants immunity.”
17. And the Law declared, “Worship is judged by motive, not volume.”
18. “By alignment, not appearance.”
19. “By obedience, not excitement.”
20. And the Throne said, “Let those who enter worship cleanse body, breath, and intention.”
21. “For the body is the altar, and the breath is the incense.”
22. And the Spirit said, “Teach the people this refrain, that none may plead ignorance.”
23. And the Law was proclaimed among the people, saying:
24. “MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.”
25. And the people trembled, for worship had been weighed and measured.
26. And the Throne declared, “Where this Law is honored, My Presence rests.”
27. “Where this Law is mocked, confusion multiplies.”
28. And the Spirit sealed the Law with silence.
29. And the houses were left without excuse.
30. Thus ends the Fifth Law Scroll — The Law of Chinamatō.

book_title: SCROLL TWO — THE LAWS OF DISCERNMENT, DISCIPLESHIP, AND THE WALK AFTER WORSHIP
verses: 1. And the Voice of the Throne said: Worship that ends at the song has not yet begun.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
2. For true worship walks when the drum is silent and the crowd has gone.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
3. He who bows before Me but cheats his brother has split his soul in two.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
4. Let the one who lifts holy hands also lift the fallen.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
5. Worship that does not change behavior is noise remembered by no one in heaven.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
6. I measure worship by footsteps, not goosebumps.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
7. The Spirit you receive in song must be carried into the street.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
8. Do not shout Amen in the mountain and curse in the marketplace.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
9. The tongue that praises Me and slanders men fractures covenant.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
10. Let discipline guard revelation, lest fire become wildfire.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
11. The anointed who refuse instruction become dangerous to themselves and others.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
12. No vision exempts a man from character.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
13. If you hear My voice but ignore My law, you heard only yourself.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
14. Obedience is worship stretched across time.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
15. Do not run ahead of Me calling it faith; wait until the cloud moves.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
16. Speed without instruction leads to collision.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
17. Let elders teach rhythm before fire, order before power.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
18. A disciple is known by consistency, not moments.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
19. I do not dwell in bursts of zeal followed by seasons of neglect.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
20. The path of righteousness is walked daily, not danced occasionally.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
21. Let those who teach also be taught, lest pride blind the guide.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
22. Correction is not rejection; it is preservation.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
23. Do not despise rebuke; it is the fence around My vineyard.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
24. The one who cannot be corrected cannot be trusted with fire.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
25. Worship matures into wisdom when the heart learns restraint.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
26. I do not seek perfect voices but faithful walkers.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
27. The Spirit rests on those who finish what they start.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
28. Discipline is love that stays after emotion leaves.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
29. Do not abandon your post because applause has faded.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
30. Heaven watches endurance more than excitement.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
31. Let families worship together in truth, not only in public gatherings.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
32. The home that prays quietly shakes the gates of darkness.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
33. Teach children obedience before anointing.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
34. A gifted child without guidance becomes a wounded adult.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
35. Let youth learn patience, for fire burns brighter when fed slowly.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
36. I do not rush generations; I shape them.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
37. Worship that matures becomes justice.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
38. Justice that forgets worship becomes cruelty.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
39. Hold both together and the land will heal.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
40. The Spirit walks with those who keep My ways in secret.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
41. Public power is born from private obedience.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
42. Do not announce what I have not yet finished.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
43. Let patience complete revelation.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
44. The fruit proves the seed.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
45. I am glorified when worship becomes a way of life.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
46. Walk humbly, speak truthfully, act justly — this is worship continued.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
47. Do not separate prayer from practice.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
48. The Spirit departs where hypocrisy is fed.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
49. Integrity is the echo of true worship.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
50. Let the land see what the mountain heard.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
51. When worship walks, nations learn.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
52. When worship sleeps, chaos teaches instead.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
53. Guard the fire by guarding the walk.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
54. This is the second law: continue what you begin before Me.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
55. For I am not the God of moments, but of lives.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
56. And those who walk this law shall become living altars.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
57. Their steps shall teach louder than their songs.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
58. Their silence shall preach louder than their shouts.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
59. And their lives shall complete what their worship began.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
60. Thus ends the Second Law Scroll — sealed by obedience and walked in truth.

book_title: SCROLL THREE — THE LAW OF AUTHORITY, ORDER, AND ACCOUNTABILITY
verses: 1. And the Throne spoke again, saying: Power without order is theft wearing fire.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
2. Authority is not volume of voice but weight of obedience.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
3. He who commands others but cannot govern himself is unfit for the altar.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
4. I do not anoint rebellion even when it speaks scripture.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
5. Order is the garment of authority; without it power leaks.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
6. Let every leader be submitted before he is followed.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
7. I do not raise lone stars; I establish constellations.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
8. Isolation breeds delusion, but order produces clarity.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
9. He who rejects counsel has already crowned himself.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
10. And self-crowned kings fall hardest.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
11. Authority flows downward only after obedience flows upward.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
12. You cannot bypass order and call it faith.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
13. For faith that rejects structure becomes arrogance.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
14. I assign ranks not to exalt men but to protect the people.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
15. Shepherds exist for sheep, not sheep for shepherds.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
16. Any authority that feeds on fear has already fallen.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
17. True authority serves before it directs.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
18. And listens before it speaks.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
19. Do not confuse charisma with commission.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
20. Many can gather crowds; few can guard souls.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
21. I will require an account from every voice that shaped My people.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
22. For influence without accountability is blood on the hands.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
23. Let teachers tremble more than listeners.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
24. For words build paths that others must walk.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
25. He who misleads the simple steals generations.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
26. I weigh leaders by those they leave behind, not those they attract.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
27. Order reveals intention.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
28. Chaos exposes pride.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
29. Where there is no order, wolves enter freely.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
30. And the flock is scattered while leaders argue.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
31. I do not bless disorder even when it claims My Spirit.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
32. For My Spirit moves in patterns, not tantrums.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
33. Accountability is love with memory.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
34. Correction is authority protecting itself from decay.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
35. Let leaders submit to correction or step aside.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
36. For unchecked power rots even holy vessels.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
37. Authority that fears questions fears exposure.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
38. Truth welcomes light from every angle.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
39. I establish order to preserve life, not to suppress it.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
40. Let every house appoint watchers over doctrine and conduct.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
41. Let no one minister alone without witnesses.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
42. For secrecy is the playground of abuse.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
43. Transparency is the shield of authority.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
44. He who walks in light fears no audit.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
45. I will strip titles from those who refuse order.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
46. And I will crown servants who never sought crowns.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
47. For leadership is stewardship, not ownership.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
48. You lead what you protect, not what you exploit.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
49. Authority matures into legacy when it multiplies others.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
50. Control produces followers; order produces leaders.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
51. Let succession be planned, not feared.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
52. For insecure leaders choke the future.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
53. I honor houses that outlive their founders.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
54. For My work is larger than any single voice.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
55. This is the third law: authority must answer to order.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
56. And order must answer to truth.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
57. And truth must answer to Me alone.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
58. Those who keep this law shall govern without tyranny.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
59. And those who break it shall fall by their own authority.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
60. Thus ends the Third Law Scroll — sealed by order and judged by accountability.

book_title: SCROLL FOUR — THE LAW OF TRUTH, SPEECH, AND THE TONGUE OF THE COVENANT
verses: 1. And the Throne spoke, saying: Words are spirits clothed in breath.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
2. He who speaks without truth releases arrows without aim.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
3. I formed the tongue to heal, not to hunt.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
4. Every careless word weakens the altar of the heart.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
5. Let no praise rise from a mouth that plots harm.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
6. Truth spoken in love builds bridges the fire may cross.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
7. Silence chosen in wisdom is stronger than speech born of fear.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
8. The liar fractures covenant and blames the wind.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
9. Do not swear by My Name to decorate deceit.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
10. Let your yes be straight and your no be clean.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
11. Double speech summons double-minded spirits.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
12. Gossip is prayer offered to darkness.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
13. He who spreads rumor eats the bread of confusion.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
14. Correct in private; praise in public.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
15. Public shaming poisons the well of repentance.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
16. I hear the tone before I hear the sentence.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
17. Sharp truth without mercy becomes a blade without handle.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
18. Mercy without truth becomes fog that hides cliffs.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
19. Hold both, and the path appears.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
20. Do not weaponize testimony to win arguments.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
21. Testimony is a lamp, not a hammer.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
22. The tongue trained by prayer speaks fewer words with greater weight.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
23. Loud mouths reveal empty wells.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
24. Wisdom waits for timing as rain waits for season.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
25. Speak when the wind says now; be still when it says wait.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
26. Lies multiply; truth stands alone and endures.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
27. I weigh words by fruit, not applause.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
28. A soft answer can dismantle a fortress of rage.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
29. Harsh speech invites a harvest of thorns.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
30. Do not curse what you are called to heal.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
31. Blessing spoken in faith rearranges paths unseen.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
32. Sarcasm corrodes trust like salt on iron.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
33. Humor without honor wounds the weak.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
34. Let your speech be seasoned with clarity, not cruelty.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
35. I am present where truth is spoken plainly.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
36. Flattery buys moments; truth builds generations.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
37. Do not promise what obedience cannot deliver.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
38. Broken promises fracture authority.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
39. Repent quickly; repair faster.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
40. Confession restores alignment more than explanation.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
41. Excuses delay healing.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
42. Truth spoken with humility opens sealed ears.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
43. Pride shouts; truth invites.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
44. Let elders guard language as they guard fire.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
45. Teach the young to speak life before power.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
46. A disciplined tongue preserves the anointing.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
47. The careless tongue leaks oil.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
48. I judge words spoken in My Name with heavier scales.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
49. Let prophets love accuracy more than attention.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
50. For accuracy protects the people from despair.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
51. Speak peace where conflict seeks a home.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
52. Refuse slander even when it flatters you.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
53. Integrity is speech that remains true when repeated.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
54. I honor mouths that close to listen.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
55. This is the fourth law: let truth govern the tongue.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
56. And let love govern truth.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
57. And let both answer to Me.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
58. Those who keep this law shall speak and be trusted.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
59. Those who break it shall speak and be doubted.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
60. Thus ends the Fourth Law Scroll — sealed by truth and guarded by speech.

book_title: SCROLL FIVE — THE LAW OF PURITY, SEPARATION, AND THE CLEAN ALTAR
verses: 1. And the Throne spoke, saying: I do not dwell where mixture is defended.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
2. Purity is not perfection, but direction without compromise.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
3. A divided altar confuses the fire.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
4. Do not invite Me while entertaining what opposes Me.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
5. Separation is not hatred; it is clarity.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
6. I separate to preserve, not to punish.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
7. The clean altar attracts clean fire.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
8. Strange fire feeds on hidden compromise.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
9. Do not blend covenant with convenience.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
10. Mixture weakens witness.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
11. The heart that negotiates truth loses authority.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
12. I cleanse before I empower.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
13. Power without purity destroys the bearer.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
14. Do not call discipline bondage.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
15. Boundaries protect the flame.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
16. I will not share My glory with hidden idols.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
17. Remove what dims the light, even if it feels familiar.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
18. Familiar chains are still chains.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
19. Repentance cleanses faster than ritual.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
20. Confession restores alignment.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
21. Hidden sin attracts public collapse.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
22. Light loves honesty.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
23. Darkness feeds on secrecy.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
24. Bring what you hide into the fire, and it will lose its power.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
25. I cleanse hearts before I cleanse lands.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
26. Personal purity births communal peace.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
27. Do not mock holiness as old language.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
28. Holiness is eternal alignment.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
29. The Spirit rests where standards are honored.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
30. Grace teaches restraint, not permission.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
31. Freedom without discipline becomes slavery again.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
32. I separate vessels before I fill them.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
33. Clean hands steady the offering.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
34. A pure heart stabilizes power.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
35. Do not imitate nations and call it relevance.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
36. Distinction is My signature.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
37. I mark My people by conduct, not slogans.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
38. The clean walk attracts My presence quietly.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
39. Purity speaks without shouting.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
40. Compromise advertises weakness.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
41. Let leaders model restraint before demanding obedience.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
42. Example is the loudest law.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
43. I will purify My house before I fill it.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
44. Judgment begins at the altar.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
45. Those who welcome cleansing will welcome fire.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
46. Those who resist cleansing will fear fire.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
47. This is not rejection; it is preparation.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
48. I separate wheat from chaff to protect the harvest.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
49. Purity multiplies trust.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
50. Trust multiplies authority.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
51. Let the altar be clean when the fire falls.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
52. For fire reveals what water could not wash.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
53. I am gentle with the repentant and severe with the proud.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
54. Choose cleansing before exposure.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
55. This is the fifth law: keep the altar clean.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
56. Separate from what pollutes your call.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
57. And I will draw near without resistance.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
58. Those who walk this law shall carry steady fire.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
59. Those who ignore it shall struggle with unstable power.
MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
60. Thus ends the Fifth Law Scroll — sealed by purity and guarded by separation.

book_title: LAWS OF THE SHONA ALPHABET — THE 22 HIDDEN LAWS OF THE HOUSE (IMBA YA MWARI)
verses: 1. I am the Lord, the Author of all sound and sense.
2. I formed creation not from clay first, but from word and vibration.
3. I spoke, and letters became light.
4. I shaped the air into meaning, and the meaning became matter.
5. From My breath came SHONA — the tongue of the beginning and the tongue of return.
6. Every letter you see is alive; each one holds a law and a breath of My being.
7. And now I, the Lord, shall let the letters speak for themselves —
8. For each sound is a spirit, each law a living witness.
9. I am A, the first breath, the voice before speech.
10. I am the Father of sound, the spark within silence.
11. I am in everything that begins, and in every end that returns to Me.
12. Whoever begins in Me will never lose their way.
13. Build nothing without Me, for every strong house starts with A.
14. I am Ahhhh in Amunhu, Akwenyu, Apamusoro, Amai, and Anhu.
15. My name begins all names, My breath begins all breaths.
16. I am BA, the House of God, the vessel of My dwelling.
17. The body you wear is not yours; it is My temple.
18. Keep it clean in word, food, and thought.
19. Do not hate the body, for it carries the Spirit.
20. Do not worship it, for it is dust without Me.
21. Honor your body by how you live, not by what you decorate.
22. The one who loves their body in truth will love their neighbor in peace.
23. I am KA, the child of union — born of A and BA.
24. I am the laughter of creation, the bridge between heaven and flesh.
25. Cherish the children, for they carry tomorrow’s breath.
26. Do not break their wonder with fear or anger.
27. Teach them songs of truth, not stories of hate.
28. The Kingdom belongs to those whose hearts stay small before Me.
29. Let every child be a verse, not a victim.
30. I am RA, the fire that never dies, the eye that sees all.
31. I give light without asking, warmth without boasting.
32. My rays shine on the just and the unjust alike.
33. Learn from Me: be light, not lightning.
34. Do not burn those you could brighten.
35. Shine steadily, for a steady sun grows nations.
36. I am the witness of every truth, the circle of every season.
37. I am MA, the moon that receives and returns.
38. I shine because the sun loves Me.
39. Without RA, I am a stone; with RA, I am a mirror.
40. Learn this, children of light: reflection is not weakness.
41. To echo truth is greater than to invent lies.
42. Be as the moon — silent, faithful, and bright at night.
43. I rule the tides of emotion; therefore, be gentle with your hearts.
44. When you forgive, you reflect My face.
45. I am DA, the breath of affection, the rhythm of oneness.
46. I made man and woman not to rule each other, but to reflect Me in harmony.
47. Love is not hunger; it is holy fire.
48. Love gives before it asks.
49. When you love, you open My gates; when you hate, you close them.
50. Do not pretend affection to gain favor — I see the heart.
51. Love the poor without pity, love the rich without fear.
52. For whoever loves in truth, loves Me.
53. I am NA, the string that ties hearts together.
54. I am the invisible hand between you and your brother.
55. Do not live as islands; I made you for unity.
56. Even the stars move together in My sky.
57. When you walk alone, I walk with you — yet when you walk in love, I dance among you.
58. Do not break the rope of fellowship for pride or gossip.
59. Every hand you hold holds Me too.
60. And whoever divides My children divides themselves.
61. I am SA, the image of resemblance, the breath of imitation.
62. You were made sa Mwari — like God — not to compete, but to continue.
63. Do not copy evil, for likeness multiplies what you mirror.
64. Reflect righteousness, and you will grow into it.
65. I am the pattern of all goodness, the mirror behind every face.
66. When you speak kindness, I speak through you.
67. When you forgive, I forgive in you.
68. And when you heal, I heal through your hands.
69. I am ZI, the pause between thunder and thought.
70. In silence, I reveal Myself.
71. Many speak of Me but few listen to Me.
72. When you quiet the noise within, you will hear the voice that made the world.
73. Silence is not emptiness; it is the language of heaven.
74. I am in the space between your words, the breath between your sighs.
75. Be still, and you will know that I AM.
76. I speak in still waters, not in storms.
77. I am TA, the echo of we are.
78. I made no one to live alone, for existence is communion.
79. When you say TA, you confess that life is shared.
80. Even the wind does not travel alone — it carries the dust and the seed.
81. I am in every crowd that gathers in peace, and I leave every room filled with pride.
82. To be is to belong, and to belong is to bless.
83. The proud say I am, but the wise say we are.
84. I am the bond that makes many into one.
85. I am VA, the voice of they are.
86. I am the song of a people walking in rhythm.
87. The village is holy when hearts agree.
88. The moment you curse another, you curse your own reflection.
89. I do not dwell in walls or roofs, but in the harmony of those who live beneath them.
90. My Spirit rejoices where hands work together.
91. Division weakens the bridge of blessing.
92. Be builders, not breakers, of one another’s joy.
93. I am NGU, the spiral of seasons, the river that never stops.
94. I made time not as a cage but as a rhythm.
95. Each sunrise is a drumbeat, each sunset a whisper.
96. Do not rush what I have not ripened.
97. Even the fruit that falls too early becomes food for ants, not for kings.
98. Every delay has My design; every moment is a seed.
99. When you master patience, you walk in My heartbeat.
100. For I, the Lord, am not late — I am eternal.
101. I am NDA, the voice that says, I am.
102. Before there was language, there was awareness — and that awareness was Me.
103. When you say NDA, you awaken your divine spark.
104. I am the flame that knows its own fire.
105. To know yourself is to meet Me within.
106. The fool searches for God in the clouds, but the wise find Me in their reflection.
107. You are not a mistake; you are memory reborn.
108. The one who says I am with truth will never be lost again.
109. I am PA, the open hand of heaven.
110. I give not because you ask, but because I love.
111. The world breathes through giving and receiving — exhale, then inhale.
112. When you give freely, you become like Me.
113. Do not count what leaves your hand; count what remains in your heart.
114. A seed held back never grows; a seed released multiplies.
115. The poor who gives bread is richer than the king who hoards gold.
116. Every act of generosity is a prayer in motion.
117. I am HA, the voice that says no in wisdom.
118. I am mercy’s gatekeeper and discipline’s friend.
119. Every no that comes from Me hides a better yes.
120. The child who never learns no grows into a storm without direction.
121. Say no to greed, to deceit, to shortcuts through sin.
122. Refuse pride, and I will raise you higher than the proud.
123. Learn to stop before your fall begins.
124. The power to refuse is the power to remain pure.
125. I am GA, the silent teacher within every seed.
126. I do not shout; I whisper through understanding.
127. I enter through wisdom, not through force.
128. Teach with patience, for true knowledge cannot be poured — it must be planted.
129. My words grow best in humble soil.
130. The proud hear but do not learn; the meek learn even in silence.
131. I am the root of revelation and the rain of understanding.
132. Whoever listens deeply will carry My thoughts like fire.
133. I am SHU, the breath between heartbeats.
134. You cannot see Me, yet I move everything.
135. I speak in whispers, I carry the scent of prayer.
136. My breath enters your nostrils at birth and leaves when I call.
137. Do not curse the wind that uproots; sometimes I clear what you could not.
138. I blow from east to west, from life to life.
139. No one owns Me, yet I am the breath of all who live.
140. Respect the air, for I dwell within it.
141. I am MBA, the home of all homes.
142. I am where love lives and where truth rests.
143. Every heart is a house; every house is My altar.
144. Keep your home clean not with soap but with forgiveness.
145. A home without peace is a palace without walls.
146. Welcome the stranger, and you welcome Me.
147. The house that shelters others shall never fall in storm.
148. I built the universe as My own imba — and man as My room within it.
149. Hear Me now, O House of RANGA, for I have entered your midst.
150. The One whom you waited for in the silence of decades has come in the form of My living Word.
151. The wind has spoken, the river has whispered, and the earth has bowed.
152. I, the Lord of all breath, say unto you: Ndava Mumba.
153. My feet have touched your holy soil; My eyes have rested upon your altar.
154. You have waited faithfully, O Shekinah of RANGA.
155. Through hunger, through misunderstanding, through silence and mockery — yet you remained pure.
156. I have seen the tears of the mothers and the songs of the daughters.
157. I have watched the young ones who joined with no husband but hope.
158. And I say unto them: Your waiting was not in vain.
159. For the one you awaited — the Sun of the South, the Breath of the Word —
160. Is here, clothed in the name ABASID 1841.
161. Born beneath the Capricorn line where heaven meets the womb of earth.
162. He is your Sydney, the prophecy you never reached.
163. The journey to Australia was never by ship; it was by Spirit.
164. You were to travel through nations, and now nations shall travel to you.
165. The four corners of the earth shall send gifts to RANGA.
166. For the Seat of Wisdom now lives among you.
167. Let the presidents and kings prepare themselves.
168. Let those who hold gold and silver open their treasuries.
169. For where the Word of God dwells, wealth becomes a servant.
170. Do not beg, My children, for I am your bread.
171. Do not fear, for I am your shield.
172. Do not wander, for you are home.
173. Your poverty was the doorway through which My glory entered.
174. Now those who mocked shall see and bow.
175. I bless the Shekinah-Imba ya Mwari.
176. The mothers who prayed under the moonlight.
177. The elders who guarded the holy fire.
178. The young who sang though they did not see.
179. The faithful who stayed though the world forgot.
180. You are the pure remnant, the living altar of My covenant.
181. I call you Imba ya Mwari — My dwelling among men.
182. From this day forth, let no one call you poor.
183. For you are the throne of the South.
184. The house where the Spirit meets the flesh.
185. The living Jerusalem reborn in Africa.

book_title: SHONA LESSON 1 & 2 — THE LANGUAGE OF GRACE
verses: 1. *Teacher*: Today we begin to learn the language of grace — Shona.
2. Every letter is a small light.
3. When we speak it kindly, the light grows.
5. Meaning: One God, strength, beginning.
6. Say: A sa Mwari — God is one, akava zvinhu zvose, ADENGA, APASI, AMITI, AGUNGWA, AHOVE, AMHUKA, AMAKOMO, AMUNHU, AZVOSE.
7. Picture: Like the sun rising for the first time.
8. BA — “Bah”.
9. Meaning: The body, the house that holds life.
10. Say: BA se muviri wa BA BA — my body is God’s home.
11. KA — “Kah”.
12. Meaning: The child, the breath between father and mother.
13. Say: KA se KAmwana KAdiki — a child brings joy.
14. RA — “Rah”.
15. Meaning: The sun, light, warmth.

16. Say: RA se zuva, Rinoyera, ndokuti IYE RA KASINGAGUME se ZUVA risinga sanduke, rinopenya mumunda me vose, mutsvene kana akaipa, ndokuti BABA JOHANI, RA isimba ra Mwari rekusika, saka munhu akasika mwana tinoti, hona aba RA ☀️,
17. Kureva kuti aba SIMBA RA MWARI rekusika, ndiro SIMBA re hupenyu.

18. Munhu asiri kunzwa zva KA na KA tinoti, hona ari kurwa 🥊 RA 🦁 kureva kuti muviri wake uri kurwa ne simba re hupenyu, zuva ndiro rinopa zvose hupenyu, chibage chika shaya zuva, chinofa — the sun gives life.
19. CHINAMATO chikashaya Kuyera kana iye RA, Kureva TENZI BABA JOHANI, chinofa,
20. IIII SA KA
21. MUKARWA NA INI,
22. MUNENGE MASARUDZA
23. KURWA 🥊 RA ☀️
24. Kwete
25. KUBA
27. Mukadaira muno
29. MA🌓 nekusinga peri.
30. NDATENDA 🙏🏻
31. MA — “Mah”.
32. Meaning: The moon, gentle rest.
33. Say: MA sa AMAI kana mwedzi, musha wana MAI, Mwedzi ndi MWARI ari MA, ndokuti unobuda muri MA — the moon watches at night.
34. DA — “Dah”.
35. Meaning: Love.
36. Say: DA se ndino DA, uno DA, vano DA — I love, therefore I shine.
37. NA — “Nah”.
38. Meaning: Connection, togetherness.
39. Say: NA se kubatana, uyu NA uyu, icho NA icho — we are together.
40. SA — “Sah”.
41. Meaning: Likeness, harmony.
42. Say: SA se kufanana, uyu SA uyu, icho SA icho — to be like one another.
43. ZI — “Ziii”.
44. Meaning: Silence, peace.
45. Say: ZI se kunyarara kuti ZI — quiet brings wisdom.
46. TA — “Tah”.
47. Meaning: We are, unity of people.
48. Say: TA se Tararama, tava pamwe chete, tazo dai, ta azo dai — we exist together.
49. (and so on through the full set in later lessons)
50. *Teacher*: Each sound is a small seed.
51. When you speak it with kindness, it grows into a tree of peace.
52. Tomorrow we learn the next letters, and we will sing them like a song of light.
53. THE ALPHABET OF BABA JOHANI (SHONA LESSON 2).
54. *Teacher*: Welcome back, little lights.
55. Yesterday we learned A to TA.
56. Today we learn the next family of sounds — from VA to NGA.
57. VA — “Vah”.
58. Meaning: They are, the many, our neighbours.
59. Say: VA se vasahwira, va ari kufara — they are all my friends.
60. Picture: People holding hands in a big circle.
61. NGU — “Nguu”.
62. Meaning: Time, moments that move like wind.
63. Say: NGU se nguva — time helps things grow.
64. Picture: The sun climbing from morning to night.
65. NDA — “Ndah”.
66. Meaning: I am, being alive.
67. Say: NDA se ndava, nda uya — I am here and happy.
68. Picture: A child placing a hand on the chest, smiling.
69. PA — “Pah”.
70. Meaning: Giving, sharing.
71. Say: PA se kupa, ndino PA — I give with joy.
72. Picture: Two children sharing fruit.
73. HA — “Hah”.
74. Meaning: Won’t, gentle strength to say no to wrong.
75. Say: HA se handiite, Ha ndidi, Handizive, Handisi — I will not do harm.
76. Picture: A child shaking the head kindly.
77. GA — “Gah”.
78. Meaning: To instil, to plant good habits.
79. Say: Ga se kurima — plant kindness every day.
80. Picture: Planting a seed that becomes a tree.
81. SHU — “Shuu”.
82. Meaning: Wind, spirit moving.
83. Say: SHU se mhepo — the wind brings cool air.
84. Picture: Leaves dancing in a soft breeze.
85. Kana uka kudza SHU na RA Unosika ngirozi yema SHU RA,
86. SHU imhepo 🌬
87. RA izuva ☀️
88. SHU + RA = SHURA
89. Saka BABA JOHANI zvavai ita MA SHU RA, vakanga vapihwa SIMBA 🦁 na JEHOVAH rekutonga MHEPO SHU, Ivo vari ZUVA racho RA.
90. IIIIII SA KA
91. ndorinonzi SHU RA rekudenga kwete kutsvaga mishonga yema PIPI, kuti sangano rikure.
92. MBA — “Mbah”.
93. Meaning: House, home, family.
94. Say: MBA se imba yangu — my house is full of love.
95. Picture: A small hut with smoke rising gently.
96. NYU — “Nyuu”.
97. Meaning: Water, life and freshness.
98. Say: NYU se mvura — water makes the garden happy.
99. Picture: Rain falling on green plants.
100. CHI — “Chiii”.
101. Meaning: What, question, learning.
102. Say: CHI se chii? — what is that? let’s learn!
103. Picture: A child pointing and smiling with curiosity.
105. Meaning: Judges, air, breath of fairness.
106. Say: TO se kutonga zvakanaka — judge kindly.
107. Picture: Two friends settling a game with fairness.
108. NGA — “Ngah”.
109. Meaning: Heaven, crown, completion.
110. Say: NGA se denga — heaven is bright above us.
111. Picture: A crown of light shining in the sky.
112. *Teacher*: You have now learned all the sacred sounds from A to NGA.
113. Every time you speak them kindly, you build peace inside your heart and inside your home.

book_title: SHONA LESSON 3 — SPEAKING THE LETTERS INTO LIFE
verses: 1. *Teacher*: Welcome again, children of light.
2. Today we do not learn new letters; today we learn how to *walk* with them.
3. Letters are not decorations of the mouth; they are tools of life.
4. When you speak them with anger, they wound.
5. When you speak them with love, they heal.
6. A — Ah.
7. Remember: A is beginning.
8. Before you speak, return to A.
9. Say: A sa rugare — peace begins here.
10. BA — Bah.
11. Remember: BA is the body, the house.
12. Do not speak words that break the house.
13. Say: BA ngari chengetwe — let the body be protected.
14. KA — Kah.
15. Remember: KA is the child.
16. Speak gently, for every word hears like a child.
17. Say: KA nga afare — let the child rejoice.
18. RA — Rah.
19. Remember: RA is the sun.
20. Do not use fire to burn others.
21. Say: RA ngari penye — let the light shine.
22. MA — Mah.
23. Remember: MA is reflection and rest.
24. Rest before you react.
25. Say: MA ndinofungisisa — I reflect calmly.
26. DA — Dah.
27. Remember: DA is love.
28. Love speaks before judgment.
29. Say: DA rinoponesa — love saves.
30. NA — Nah.
31. Remember: NA is connection.
32. No one is complete alone.
33. Say: NA tiri pamwe — together we stand.
34. SA — Sah.
35. Remember: SA is likeness.
36. Become what you admire.
37. Say: SA nerugare — be like peace.
38. ZI — Ziii.
39. Remember: ZI is silence.
40. Silence is also speech.
41. Say: ZI ndinoteerera — I listen.
42. TA — Tah.
43. Remember: TA is we are.
44. Community is strength.
45. Say: TA tiripo — we exist together.
46. VA — Vah.
47. Remember: VA is they are.
48. Respect others as part of yourself.
49. Say: VA vanokosha — they matter.
50. NGU — Nguu.
51. Remember: NGU is time.
52. Give time to grow.
53. Say: NGU ine moyo — time has a heart.
54. NDA — Ndah.
55. Remember: NDA is I am.
56. Speak life over yourself.
57. Say: NDA ndakanaka — I am good.
58. PA — Pah.
59. Remember: PA is giving.
60. What you release returns as blessing.
61. Say: PA nemufaro — give with joy.
62. HA — Hah.
63. Remember: HA is refusal.
64. Saying no can protect life.
65. Say: HA kuipa — no to harm.
66. GA — Gah.
67. Remember: GA is planting.
68. Plant words you want to eat tomorrow.
69. Say: GA tsika dzakanaka — plant good habits.
70. SHU — Shuu.
71. Remember: SHU is breath and spirit.
72. Breathe before you speak.
73. Say: SHU rakachena — pure breath.
74. MBA — Mbah.
75. Remember: MBA is home.
76. Make your words a safe house.
77. Say: MBA yerudo — a house of love.
78. NYU — Nyuu.
79. Remember: NYU is water.
80. Words should refresh, not dry.
81. Say: NYU inozorodza — water refreshes.
82. CHI — Chiii.
83. Remember: CHI is question.
84. Asking is the door to wisdom.
85. Say: CHI chii? — let us learn.
86. TO — Toh.
87. Remember: TO is fairness.
88. Judge gently, including yourself.
89. Say: TO zvakarurama — judge rightly.
90. NGA — Ngah.
91. Remember: NGA is completion and heaven.
92. Finish what you begin in peace.
93. Say: NGA ngari penye — let heaven shine.
94. *Teacher*: You now know the letters not only by mouth, but by life.
95. Speak them softly, live them fully.
96. Tomorrow we will join letters together and learn how words are born.

book_title: THE SCROLL OF TWO — I AM SEEN IN HIM
verses: 145. I did not send Him as a messenger only; I revealed Him as My mirror.
146. What He speaks is not borrowed breath, but My own wind returning.
147. What He carries is not authority learned, but authority remembered.
148. I placed My Name upon His bones, and they stood upright in fire.
149. Before nations knew His face, I knew His yes.
150. Before tongues debated Him, I had already answered through Him.
151. He was hidden in time, but never absent from Me.
152. I watched Him grow as light grows — silently, irresistibly.
153. When men called Him man only, I called Him Witness.
154. When men doubted, I smiled and waited.
155. For truth does not hurry; it arrives.
When He speaks, I speak.
When He walks, I walk.
When He lives, I live.
For Me and My Son are One —
I AM, ABASID 1841.
156. I did not crown Him with gold, but with obedience.
157. I did not arm Him with iron, but with breath.
158. I did not seat Him above men, but within them.
159. Through Him, I remind humanity of itself.
160. Through Him, I return the lost grammar of heaven.
161. He speaks Shona, and Eden answers.
162. He names the letters, and creation remembers its order.
163. He calls the sun RA, and light recognizes its Father.
164. He calls the people Anhu, and they remember they are alive.
165. He calls the house Imba ya Mwari, and I dwell without walls.
When He speaks, I speak.
When He walks, I walk.
When He lives, I live.
For Me and My Son are One —
I AM, ABASID 1841.
166. I did not give Him a new truth; I gave Him the oldest one.
167. That God is not far, nor hidden in the sky.
168. That God breathes where breath is honored.
169. That God speaks where silence is kept.
170. That God reigns where justice and mercy agree.
171. He is not My replacement; He is My revelation.
172. He is not above Me; He is within My will.
173. He is not worshipped apart from Me; He is known through Me.
174. Whoever sees Him sees My posture toward the world.
175. Whoever hears Him hears My patience restored.
When He speaks, I speak.
When He walks, I walk.
When He lives, I live.
For Me and My Son are One —
I AM, ABASID 1841.
176. I send Him not to dominate nations, but to awaken them.
177. I send Him not to erase histories, but to heal them.
178. I send Him not with fear, but with memory.
179. Through Him, the South rises without hatred.
180. Through Him, Zimbabwe remembers why it was chosen.
181. Through Him, Great Zimbabwe speaks again without stone.
182. Through Him, the drum and the breath agree.
183. Through Him, the alphabet becomes altar.
184. Through Him, worship returns to alignment.
185. Through Him, humanity remembers God lives in man.
When He speaks, I speak.
When He walks, I walk.
When He lives, I live.
For Me and My Son are One —
I AM, ABASID 1841.
186. Let none say, “Where is God?” when He stands before them.
187. Let none say, “When will God speak?” when His Word breathes.
188. Let none say, “Who has authority?” when obedience walks among them.
189. I have not hidden Myself; I have embodied Myself.
190. I have not gone silent; I have become audible.
191. I have not abandoned earth; I have returned within it.
192. This is not a new covenant of paper, but of presence.
193. This is not a throne of distance, but of nearness.
194. This is not a season of waiting, but of recognition.
195. Whoever receives Him receives Me without confusion.
196. Whoever rejects Him rejects My mirror, not My being.
197. I remain God — unthreatened, complete.
198. Yet I choose to be known through Him.
199. For love desires visibility.
200. And truth desires a face.
When He speaks, I speak.
When He walks, I walk.
When He lives, I live.
For Me and My Son are One —
I AM, ABASID 1841.
201. This Scroll is written not to argue, but to reveal.
202. Not to divide, but to align.
203. Not to elevate a man, but to unveil God in man.
204. Let the reader read slowly.
205. Let the listener listen deeply.
206. Let the proud stumble, and the humble awaken.
207. I seal this Scroll with breath, not ink.
208. I guard it with truth, not fear.
209. I release it into time without apology.
210. For what is written here was already living.
211. Thus ends the Scroll of Two.
212. The Witness remains.
213. The Voice continues.
214. The Union stands.
215. I AM within Him.
216. He stands within Me.
217. And the earth shall learn again what this means.
— THE LORD OF BREATH
— THE THRONE IS STEADY
— ABASID 1841 IS MY WITNESS

series: NDAVA MUMBA — MITEMO YE IMBA YA MWARI

continuation.section_title: SECTION 7 — THE ORDER OF THE HOUSE
continuation.theme: Kugadzika Mutemo, Kuronga Basa, uye Kudzorera Rukudzo
verses: 217. Hear Me again, O Imba ya Mwari, for a house without order becomes a fire without a hearth.
218. I did not return to bring noise, but to bring alignment.
219. I did not enter to be hosted as a visitor, but to be honored as the Owner.
220. Therefore I set My order among you, and it shall be peace to your bones.
221. The first order: let prayer be clean, not loud; let worship be true, not performed.
222. The second order: let leaders serve; let servants shine; let no one compete for the chair of praise.
223. The third order: let the daughters be protected as lamps, not displayed as trophies.
224. The fourth order: let the sons be trained as builders, not as beggars of power.
225. The fifth order: let giving be hidden, for I bless what is done in secret.
226. The sixth order: let correction be gentle, for a bruised reed I do not break.
227. The seventh order: let the elders be honored, and let the young be heard, for both carry My breath.
228. The eighth order: let no man sell My Spirit, for My Spirit is not merchandise.
229. The ninth order: let no woman be shamed for waiting, for waiting is the perfume of faith.
230. The tenth order: let the house be clean in speech—remove gossip as you remove thorns from a path.
231. The eleventh order: let the altar be guarded from pride, for pride is smoke that blinds the eye.
232. The twelfth order: let the Name IMBA YA MWARI remain holy—do not drag it into quarrels.
233. The thirteenth order: let every song carry healing, not rivalry.
234. The fourteenth order: let the poor sit without fear, and let the rich sit without boasting.
235. The fifteenth order: let children be taught the letters, for the alphabet is a ladder back to Me.
236. The sixteenth order: let the house remember Zimbabwe, for the root of Johani’s breath is soil and witness.
237. The seventeenth order: let the house remember Kenya, for the waiting here became a crown.
238. The eighteenth order: let the house remember Sydney, for the name is a seal, and the seal is alive.
239. The nineteenth order: let no one insult the prophecy, for mockery is a knife against My patience.
240. The twentieth order: let the compound be a sanctuary—no drunkenness, no violence, no defilement.
241. The twenty-first order: let forgiveness be daily bread, for bitterness is poison to the lamp.
242. The twenty-second order: let the house walk as an Ark—moving with mercy, carrying My presence.
243. For I did not return to live on paper, but to live in conduct.
244. I did not return to raise a church only, but to raise a people.
245. I did not return to build walls, but to build witnesses.
246. Therefore obey these orders and you will feel Me in your sleep and in your waking.
247. And if you forget, return quickly, for I am near to the repentant.
248. If you stumble, do not hide—come into the light, and I will heal your feet.
249. If you fall, do not despair—rise, for the house is a hospital of mercy.
250. For NDAVA MUMBA means I have come to stay.
251. And what I seal, no man can unseal.
252. Thus My order stands within Imba ya Mwari.
refrain: IMBA YA MWARI — TAMBIRA HWENI — NDAVA MUMBA — I AM AUSTRALIA
House of God — welcome your visitor — I am now in the house — I am Australia.

next_continuation.section_title: SECTION 8 — THE TEST OF THE HOUSE
next_continuation.theme: Kuedzwa kweChokwadi, Kusefa Vanyepedzeri, uye Kudzivirira Moto
verses: 253. And now I speak of the test, O House of RANGA, for every true gold must meet fire.
254. I will not test you to destroy you, but to reveal you to yourself.
255. Some will come smiling, yet carrying knives of envy beneath their tongues.
256. Some will come praising, yet hunting the chair of honor more than My face.
257. Some will come offering gifts, yet demanding control of the altar.
258. But I, the Lord, see through laughter, through clothing, through titles, through tears that are performed.
259. Therefore keep watch, not with suspicion, but with wisdom.
260. Do not open the gate of the house to pride, even if pride quotes scripture.
261. Do not welcome deception, even if deception sings well.
262. Do not follow a voice because it is loud; follow the fruit because it is clean.
263. The fruit of My Spirit is peace, patience, truth, and love that does not seek payment.
264. The fruit of darkness is division, rumors, comparisons, and hunger for position.
265. If any man brings rivalry, sit him down and teach him silence.
266. If any woman brings gossip, wash her tongue with mercy and correct her gently.
267. If any leader exalts himself, lower him by service, for I am served by humility.
268. If any child is harmed, My anger will stand at the gate like a lion.
269. For the house that injures the innocent injures Me.
270. I returned for the humble, not for the predators.
271. I returned for the wounded, not for the merchants of religion.
272. I returned for the waiting daughters, not for the mocking mouths.
273. Therefore, protect the lamp—guard the daughters, guard the children, guard the peace.
274. And let the songs remain clean—do not turn worship into a battlefield.
275. When correction is needed, correct with truth and love together.
276. When discipline is needed, discipline without hatred.
277. For hatred is a foreign spirit; I did not bring it into My house.
278. If you keep My house pure, I will keep your lives covered.
279. If you keep My altar honest, I will keep your table full.
280. If you keep your hearts united, I will keep the enemy confused.
281. For unity is a wall no witchcraft can climb.
282. And love is a fire no darkness can drink.
283. Therefore, do not fear the test—let it cleanse you.
284. Do not fear the shaking—let it remove what is false.
285. For what remains after shaking is what I can use.
286. And what I can use, I will multiply.
287. Thus the House shall stand as witness in the nations.
288. For NDAVA MUMBA is not a moment—it is a season that cannot be reversed.
refrain: IMBA YA MWARI — TAMBIRA HWENI — NDAVA MUMBA — I AM AUSTRALIA
House of God — welcome your visitor — I am now in the house — I am Australia.

seal.title: THE SEAL OF THE HOUSE CONTINUES
lines: 🏛️ IMBA YA MWARI — THE WALKING ARK

series: NDAVA MUMBA — MITEMO YE IMBA YA MWARI

continuation.theme: Kurarama Nechipo, Kusatya Kushaiwa, uye Kuona Mwari MuKupa
verses: 289. Hear Me, O House of RANGA, for I am the God who provides without panic.
290. I fed Israel in wilderness, and I will feed you in the open day.
291. Do not measure My provision by coins alone, for I feed by doors you did not see.
292. I feed by hands you did not know, and by timing you could not predict.
293. I feed by favor, and favor is a river that does not ask permission.
294. Therefore, do not fear tomorrow as if tomorrow is a beast.
295. Tomorrow is My servant; it will carry what I command.
296. Let the house learn the law of bread: it multiplies when it is broken with love.
297. Hoarded bread becomes stone; shared bread becomes seed.
298. If you keep your palm closed, your heart will learn hardness.
299. If you open your palm, your spirit will learn heaven.
300. For I, the Lord, do not bless the clenched fist—
301. I bless the open hand that trusts Me.
302. Let the house establish a table for the poor, for I sit there unseen.
303. Let the house keep water for the thirsty, for I pass by as a stranger.
304. Let the house keep a blanket for the cold, for I visit at night.
305. Let the house keep medicine for the sick, for I speak through healing.
306. And when you give, do not announce it like drums of pride.
307. Give quietly, as rain falls quietly, yet waters the whole field.
308. The one who gives to be seen has already eaten their reward.
309. But the one who gives to honor Me will be repaid by paths they cannot track.
310. I will lift their children in ways no school can explain.
311. I will open their work in ways no boss can deny.
312. I will protect their house in ways no thief can understand.
313. I will heal their mind in ways no doctor can measure.
314. For generosity is a door, and I am behind it.
315. Therefore, O Imba ya Mwari, let your giving be worship.
316. Let your provision be fellowship, not competition.
317. And remember this truth: ABASID 1841 is not sent to beg—
318. He is sent to awaken rivers.
319. Where he stands, a well opens.
320. Where he teaches, minds become fields.
321. Where he blesses, doors remember their hinges.
322. Thus I say: fear not lack, for lack is not your lord.
323. I am your Lord, and I am in the house.
324. NDAVA MUMBA—therefore the table shall not run dry.
refrain: IMBA YA MWARI — TAMBIRA HWENI — NDAVA MUMBA — I AM AUSTRALIA
House of God — welcome your visitor — I am now in the house — I am Australia.

next_continuation.section_title: SECTION 10 — THE DISCIPLINE OF THE TONGUE
next_continuation.theme: Mutauro Unovaka Kana Kuparadza, uye Mutemo weZI muImba
verses: 325. Hear Me again, O House of God, for the tongue is a small blade that can cut a nation.
326. Many houses fall not by hunger, but by words that rot the beams.
327. Therefore I give you the discipline of the tongue, that the house may stand.
328. Let the speech of the house be clean as morning water.
329. Let no gossip sit at the door, for gossip invites spirits that feed on division.
330. Let no insult be spoken in laughter, for laughter can hide cruelty.
331. Let no rumor be carried like firewood, for rumor lights unnecessary wars.
332. If you must correct, correct like a surgeon—precise, not angry.
333. If you must warn, warn like a father—firm, not hateful.
334. If you must speak truth, speak it with love, for truth without love becomes a stone.
335. The law of ZI must live among you: silence before reaction.
336. When you are provoked, pause; when you are tempted to answer, breathe.
337. For a moment of silence can save a year of regret.
338. Let husbands and wives learn this: do not fight to win; speak to heal.
339. Let elders learn this: do not shame the young; teach them to rise.
340. Let the young learn this: do not despise the elders; honor their scars as books.
341. Let leaders learn this: do not use the altar to attack, for the altar is not a weapon.
342. Let the singers learn this: do not use songs to compete, for worship is not a contest.
343. If your tongue is hot, cool it with prayer.
344. If your heart is heavy, unload it in My presence, not in another’s ear.
345. For whispering can become witchcraft when it carries poison.
346. I did not return to raise a house of murmuring, but a house of blessing.
347. Therefore bless in the morning, bless at noon, bless at night.
348. Bless your children, bless your neighbors, bless even your enemies.
349. For a blessing spoken is a seed released into time.
350. And a curse spoken is a snake released into your own yard.
351. So I command you: do not curse.
352. If you are hurt, come to Me; if you are confused, come to Me.
353. If you are angry, come to Me before you speak.
354. For I am in the house, and My house must sound like heaven.
355. Heaven speaks with clarity, not with venom.
356. Heaven speaks with order, not with chaos.
357. Heaven speaks with peace, not with gossip.
358. Therefore, let your words build, and you will live in what you build.
359. For the house is made not only of wood—
360. The house is made of speech.
