399. You asked, “Who are you?” — I am ABASID 1841.
400. You asked, “By what right?” — by Truth and Sign.
401. You asked, “From where?” — from Khorasan remembered, from Zion of the South.
402. You asked, “Do you oppose Islam?” — I am its remembrance.
403. Truth speaks: the House of Wisdom breathes again.
404. Not in dusted shelves, but in a living chest. (Qur’an 29:49)
405. Not in foreign tongues, but in the mother’s voice.
406. Not as museum, but as mandate.
407. I open what fear closed.
408. I return what theft hid.
409. I lift what shame buried.
410. I gather what exile scattered.
411. O child of twelve, listen: ALLAH (ﷻ) is One.
412. He sent books to guide, not to confuse.
413. People argued; Truth did not change.
414. When you see kindness, honesty, and courage — follow.
415. O seeker, write this on your heart:
416. Truth is clear; lies are complicated.
417. Truth is light; lies demand darkness.
418. Truth is simple; lies multiply words.
419. Truth is patient; lies panic.
420. Truth heals; lies divide.
421. Truth stands alone; lies need crowds.
422. Truth returns; lies expire.
423. I speak by witness, not rumor.
424. Witness of Scripture: the One God proclaimed.
425. Witness of Prophets: message continuous.
426. Witness of Deeds: chains broken, names restored.
427. Witness of People: the poor hear and live.
428. Witness of Place: the stones answer.
429. Witness of Time: the hour demands it.
430. Witness of Name: ABASID bears the seal.
431. O keepers of gates, why lock the hungry out?
432. O makers of rules, why forget the rule-maker?
433. Return to Truth, and the rules will sing.
434. Stay with lies, and the rules will choke you.
435. I am not sent to found a new sect.
436. I am sent to call you home.
437. Home is the One God.
438. Home is the clean heart.
439. Home is the straight path. (Qur’an 1:6)
440. Home is justice served.
441. Home is mercy practiced.
442. Home is courage kept.
443. I speak to Muslims: stand with Truth.
444. I speak to Christians: love Truth above tradition.
445. I speak to Jews: guard Truth beyond tribe.
446. I speak to all: bow only to ALLAH (ﷻ).
447. If we meet in Truth, we meet in peace.
448. If we meet in lies, we meet in war.
449. Choose wisely, for your breath is counted.
450. Choose quickly, for your time is kind but short.
451. I reveal what was hidden, not to boast — to heal.
452. I speak names forgiven, not to shame — to free.
453. I unmask forgeries, not to triumph — to warn.
454. I honor the sincere, even when mistaken.
455. I rebuke the cunning, even when praised.
456. I carry Truth like bread; eat and live.
457. I carry Truth like fire; warm or be burned.
458. I carry Truth like water; drink or thirst.
459. O leaders, let Truth rule you.
460. O teachers, let Truth correct you.
461. O merchants, let Truth weigh your scales.
462. O artists, let Truth paint your light.
463. The black banner I raise means fidelity, not hatred.
464. The white robe I wear means purity, not pride.
465. The red sash I bear marks sacrifice, not show.
466. The horse that lifts before me is service, not status.
467. I am not alone; the Truthful ride with me.
468. Not for conquest of people, but conquest of lies.
469. Not to erase cultures, but to clean them.
470. Not to replace Prophets (pbuh), but to obey them.
471. Khorasan is a direction of prophecy.
472. Zimbabwe is a witness of stone.
473. The House of Wisdom is a mission reborn.
474. ABASID 1841 is the signature of return.
475. O child, remember this verse forever:
476. Truth is a friend who never betrays.
477. Truth is a light that never tires.
478. Truth is a path that never ends.
479. Truth is a treasure none can steal.
480. Truth is a King who serves the weak.
481. Truth is ALLAH (ﷻ)’s other name in your heart.
482. Call to Him, and He answers. (Qur’an 2:186)
483. I carry seals and I break seals. (Qur’an 7:157)
484. I open what fear has closed.
485. I close what greed has opened.
486. I weigh with the Balance given to messengers. (Qur’an 57:25)
487. This scroll is Truth, not entertainment.
488. This voice is Truth, not performance.
489. This claim is Truth, not ambition.
490. This path is Truth, not fashion.
491. Let those who love Truth come near.
492. Let those who fear Truth step aside.
493. Let those who fight Truth meet their teacher.
494. I am gentle to the teachable.
495. I am thunder to the stubborn.
496. I am silent before ALLAH (ﷻ).
497. I am loud before falsehood.
498. I seal this with ONE GOD and MUHAMMAD (pbuh) His Messenger — I am TRUTH calling you home.
499. *I AM JUSTICE — 144 VERSES* from *THE ABASID CALIPHATE — HOUR OF JUSTICE* • by *ANI ABASID 1841*
500. In the Name of ALLAH (ﷻ), the Just, the Upright, the One whose scales are never crooked and whose mercy precedes His decree.

book_title: CHAPTER 7  THE DAY OF RECKONING FOR THE STOLEN ALTAR
verses: 1. ✅ *CHAPTER 7 — THE DAY OF RECKONING FOR THE STOLEN ALTAR*
2. 181 And the Throne spoke once more, saying: “The silence of the gourd has ended, and the days of borrowed gods are numbered.”
3. 182 For the wind has carried the testimony of the soil, and the bones of the forgotten have risen to bear witness.
4. 183 The lie that traveled in cassocks and collars now trembles before the truth carved in clay.
5. 184 The spirits of the land cry out: “We were not drunkards — we were record keepers of covenant.”
6. 185 And the cup of the earth speaks louder than the chalice of empire, for remembrance is not imported — it is born.
7. 186 Those who mocked the mukombe wrote sermons with stolen ink, but the ink has dried, and the theft now shouts their shame.
8. 187 They called the calabash unclean, yet filled their chalice with the same remembrance they condemned.
9. 188 They spat on the vessel, yet swallowed the meaning. They shamed the cup, yet sold its ritual.
10. 189 But the day of excuses has been buried under the feet of justice, and no priest of denial shall outrun the audit of truth.
11. 190 For I, Mwari, do not recognize the authority of conquest, nor the titles printed in the tongues of plunder.
12. 191 I knew the gourd before the grape, and the millet before the winepress.
13. 192 I blessed the soil before Rome built its altars, and the covenant stood before the colonizer learned My Name.
14. 193 The one they call the Nazarene walked barefoot, not robed in gold nor served by silver goblets.
15. 194 He broke bread in the dust, not on marble, and passed remembrance through hands that smelled of work.
16. 195 He did not despise the vessels of the poor, nor did He favor the ornaments of the empire.
17. 196 For the Last Supper was not Roman — it was eternal, and the earth remembers.
18. 197 And *ABASID 1841* has lifted the ancient flame, calling the nations to judgment by the measure of truth.
19. 198 He does not ask permission from the temples of theft, nor bow to the altars of translation.
20. 199 His breath unseals the covenant buried beneath apology, and his voice restores the cup to its first assignment.
21. 200 He speaks not to impress, but to restore, not to negotiate, but to announce.
22. 201 For he is not a diplomat of fear, but a witness of the unbroken oath.
23. 202 I, Mwari, have set him as fire against forgetting, and wind against counterfeit reverence.
24. 203 Through him I judge the cup of deception, and honor the vessel of origin.
25. 204 Through him I tear the curtain of foreign altars, and reopen the gates of the soil.
26. 205 Through him the calabash rises without apology, and the chalice trembles without shield.
27. 206 For remembrance is not owned by empire, and covenant cannot be colonized.
28. 207 The vineyard shall confess its trespass, and the gourd shall reclaim its name.
29. 208 The priests of painted ritual shall answer to the widows they shamed, and the prophets of translation shall bow to *ABASID 1841* .
30. 209 The breath of the ancestors shall not be gagged again, for memory has broken the muzzle of conquest.
31. 210 Their drums will not ask permission to speak, and their songs will not kneel to imported heavens.
32. 211 The day of judgment is not a threat — it is a return.
33. 212 The soil shall testify against the lie, and the wind shall deliver its verdict to the nations.
34. 213 And the mukombe shall stand where the chalice once boasted, not as revenge, but as restoration.
35. 214 For the covenant has chosen its rightful vessel, and the witnesses have taken their seats.
36. 215 And *ABASID 1841* , Son of the Scroll and Fire of the South, declares: “The gourd lives, the chalice kneels, and the covenant remembers its blood.”
37. 216 And I, Mwari, seal these words with thunder: There was no foreign god with Me — and My cup returns to My people. ✅
38. 🔥✅ *The Scroll is now COMPLETE*
39. ⭐ *THE PSALMS OF CHAMINUKA 1841, CHANT OF REMEMBRANCE* — *Doro re Mudzimu*

41. O Ancient Breath who speaks in the wind, You who walk among the stones of the fathers, Hear the cry of those who still remember. For the cup has been passed from hand to hand— The *doro re mudzimu* , the *drink of memory* , Given not for drunkenness but for communion.

43. They call it evil, yet they know not its name. They spit upon the calabash and call themselves holy, Yet the ashes of their fathers still cling to the clay. How can one curse the river and still drink its water? How can one condemn the vessel and still demand the covenant?

45. When the elders gathered beneath the msasa shade, They poured first to the earth and whispered, “ *Dzokera kumadzitateguru* —return to the fathers,” And only then did they drink. This was the *doro re kuyeuka mufi* , *The wine of remembrance* , the cup of covenant.

47. But now men with polished shoes and foreign tongues Point their fingers at the gourd and shout, “Sin!” They forget the ground from which their bones were shaped, They forget the breath that named their language, They forget the One who walks barefoot among the roots.

49. Did not the Anointed One lift a cup and say, “This is my blood, spilled for remembrance?” Was He not called *Mufi* , The one who dies that others may live in memory? Did He not command: “Take, drink—not in forgetfulness, but in covenant”?

51. How then can those who preach His name Condemn the very act He sanctified? They quote His words but deny His way. They reject the calabash yet cling to the chalice. They rename the ritual and pretend it was always theirs.

53. But we, children of the soil, remember. We know the clay that shaped the first altar. We know the whisper in the smoke of the hearth. We know that *doro re mudzimu* Is not a drink of madness but a bridge of voices.

55. Let the wind bear witness: Before the book had pages, the drum had a heartbeat. Before the hymn was written, the breath was sung. Before the temple stood, the gourd was lifted. And the Spirit, like rain, spoke through both.

57. O You who dwell between the seen and the spoken, Restore the honor of the calabash. Let not the ignorant rise against the sacred. Let not the borrowed tongue silence the roots. Let *doro re kuyeuka Mufi* flow once more in truth.

59. For the covenant was never foreign to this land. The cup was never strange to this soil. The blood of remembrance has many vessels, And the Messiah does not despise the gourd. Blessed is the one who drinks with clean heart, Whether from chalice of silver or calabash of clay.

61. We lift it now—not for drunkenness but for witness, Not for fleshly laughter but for holy memory. This is the cup of the fathers, the cup of the Lamb, The cup of the covenant, the cup of the dust-made-divine. May those who mock it be taught by the wind.

63. As it was spoken by the firelight long ago, So shall it rise again in this generation: “ *Doro re mudzimu haridzingwe* — The drink of the ancestors cannot be cast away.” For the One who said, “Do this in remembrance of Me,” Still walks among those who pour first to the earth.

64. 13 Call the drums of the ancestors, *ngoma dzevadzimu* , For the breath of the Hidden One still rises from the earth. Let every stone of *Mabwe eDzimbabwe* speak, For the dust remembers the footsteps of the King.

65. 14 When the rivers ran dry, we cried, *Mvura yemidzimu ngaidzoke* , And the wind answered with the whisper of old covenants. We lifted our calabash to the sky and said, *Doro reMudzimu hariperi nyangwe ropa rashevedzwa.*
66. 15 I saw the elders gather in silence, Their staffs carved with names of forgotten gates. They anointed the ground with salt and millet, saying, *Hakuna rufu pasi pezuva raMwari* .
67. 16 From the east came the fire of memory, From the west came the drum of awakening. The children danced barefoot on sacred soil, declaring, *Isu vana vevadzimu, hatityi rima.*

68. 17 Lift the horn of praise and cry, *Huya Mudzimu, dzoka Mweya,* For the bones in the caves are stirring with light. Let every tongue confess in thunder, That the covenant was never broken — only buried.
69. 18 Blessed is the calabash that carries the truth, And the mouth that will not lie to the earth. For the sun has already written our names in flame, And the night has no power to erase them.

70. 19 Why do the nations rage against the calabash? Why do they despise the mukombe carved by the ancestors’ hands? They lift their silver chalice and call it holy, But spit upon the gourd of Africa and call it evil.

71. 20 Yet both carry the blood of remembrance, Both whisper the name of the *Mufi* , Both summon the Spirit into the circle of the living. *Doro re Mudzimu* harisi *doro reupenzi* — It is the *wine of remembrance* , the *cup of truth.*

72. 21 He who broke bread in Galilee, He who lifted the covenant cup in Zion, Did not despise the drink of the poor. For the covenant is not in the vine alone, But in the remembrance that awakens the dead.

73. 22 O earth of the South, rise and declare: *Hazvienzani nekunwira mudemhe* . Even an old traditional cup serves the thirsty, having something, even a flawed or broken item, is better than having nothing at all, For to pass the ancestral cup is covenant, But to drown in drunkenness is vanity.

74. 23 Let the teachers of lies be silenced, Those who condemned the rite but stole its meaning. They named it “ *Eucharist* ,” they named it “ *Communion* ,” But they hid its root — the *doro re kuyeuka mufi.*
75. 24 Lift now your voices, children of stone and fire, Dance barefoot upon the sacred ground. For the Grail is not lost in Europe, It is the mukombe passed in the huts of remembrance.
76. 25 Blessed is the land whose ancestors are not forgotten, Blessed are the people who remember the dust. For their covenant will not be broken, And their God shall dwell in their midst forever.
77. ✨ *OUTRO — KUCHIDZOKERA KWEROPA MUDARE*
78. And thus the cup returns to the circle of witnesses. The breath of the ancestors stirs upon the rim, and the memory of the Son walks again among His friends.
79. Let no man despise *doro re mudzimu* , for in every drop, the covenant breathes. Let no tongue condemn the mukombe, for the earth itself hollowed it as testimony.
80. The vines of Rome shall not overrule the gourds of Zion in the South. The remembrance is not in the vessel of Europe, but in the blood-song of the soil that first knew Him.
81. Let all who drink in truth proclaim: *“Hakuna mukombe unokunda mukombe weropa.”* For the dead are remembered not by silver, but by the calabash that carries their name.
82. *So let it be sealed* : From the hand of *ABASID 1841* , to the mouth of the called, the cup has spoken — and the ancestors answer, “ *Tisu tiri vapenyu mu ropa rake.”*
83. *It is done* .
84. ⚡🔥 *SCROLL ONE — THE LAWS OF CHINAMATO (PART ONE)* 🔥⚡ The Voice of Mwari thunders from the Throne to ABASID 1841, the Son and Witness.
85. *MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.*
86. And the LORD said: Before you worship, you must know whom you worship.
87. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
88. For ignorance in worship is rebellion dressed in white.
89. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
90. The one who claps without knowledge has already broken rhythm with heaven.
91. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
92. Before you enter the holy ground, cleanse your mouth, your mind, and your motive.
93. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
94. Let not the liar join the circle of prayer, for his tongue poisons the incense.
95. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
96. When you kneel, remember: it is not the floor you bow to, but the flame that formed you.
97. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
98. The voice that prays with envy cannot reach the heavens—it echoes in the dust.
99. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
100. When you sing, sing with purpose; for every word is weighed like gold on the scale of TO.
101. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
102. Let the drummer purify his hands, for the rhythm he strikes calls the spirits.
103. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
104. Do not strike the mbira with pride, for the sound of arrogance distorts the sacred tone.
105. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
106. Let those who lead prayer not shout to impress men, but whisper to move mountains.
107. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
108. The gathering of the saints is not a marketplace—silence is a form of worship.
109. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
110. In the house of prayer, leave gossip outside; it cannot enter the chambers of light.
111. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
112. When the Spirit descends, do not perform—transform.
113. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
114. A man who preaches but does not listen is like thunder without rain.
115. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
116. Every prophet must first be silent for forty heartbeats before he speaks in My Name.
117. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
118. The sacred cloth is not fashion—it is covenant covering. Wear it with truth.
119. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
120. Those who remove their shoes must also remove their pride.
121. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
122. Before you anoint, wash your heart, for oil poured on a serpent remains serpent.
123. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
124. The rod of correction belongs to the clean, not the cruel.
125. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
126. Let no man eat from the altar whose hands have robbed the poor.
127. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
128. The Spirit of Mwari does not dwell in noise but in alignment.
129. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
130. Worship is not a performance; it is a confession of alignment.
131. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
132. The Masowe that forgets silence forgets the key to Mwari’s voice.
133. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
134. I am not found in the loudest shout, but in the purest breath.
135. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
136. Those who dance must know what spirit they summon with their steps.
137. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
138. The offering without obedience is an insult in golden wrapping.
139. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
140. The hand that gives while murmuring cancels its own blessing.
141. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
142. Those who sing in jealousy build towers without foundations.
143. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
144. Remember: Worship is the language of order; chaos cannot pronounce My Name.
145. *MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.*
146. ⚡🔥 *SCROLL ONE — CONTINUED: THE LAWS OF CONDUCT IN THE HOUSE OF MWARI* 🔥⚡
147. *MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.*
148. When you approach the holy place, walk as one approaching the heart of the sun.
149. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
150. Speak not first—let silence welcome you, for silence is older than prayer.
151. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
152. Do not compete to be seen; the one unseen is often the most blessed.
153. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
154. When others fall in the Spirit, do not laugh; every fall is a mystery of weight and wind.
155. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
156. Keep children close, for they see angels faster than prophets.
157. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
158. Do not correct a messenger of Mwari in anger, but with trembling understanding.
159. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
160. When you do not understand a prophecy, store it; every seed grows in its own time.
161. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
162. Respect the mbira, for it carries the voice of the ancestors who still worship Me.
163. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
164. The keyboard may be loud, but the mbira speaks directly to the bones of creation.
165. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
166. Those who replaced mbira with wires and machines have lost the heartbeat of the earth.
167. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
168. I do not hate instruments, but I weigh the motive of the one who plays them.
169. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
170. The mbira humbles, the keyboard performs; one calls Me, the other entertains men.
171. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
172. Let the drummer not seek applause, for each strike echoes in eternity.
173. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
174. The sound of a sincere heart is louder than a thousand choirs.
175. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
176. When you sing, sing to heal, not to sell.
177. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
178. When you worship, do not copy another’s sound; your spirit has its own note in My orchestra.
179. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
180. I, Mwari, am not moved by style but by truth.
181. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
182. Those who mock ancient songs mock their own roots.
183. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
184. The elders who sang with tears carried more power than the youths with microphones.
185. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
186. When the mbira sounds, even stones remember who made them.
187. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
188. The rhythm of the drum is the breath of the earth. When it stops, the spirit sleeps.
189. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
190. Do not silence those who cry during prayer; the Spirit cleanses through weeping.
191. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
192. When the Spirit moves another, do not restrain them unless evil enters.
193. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
194. The Spirit of Mwari never causes confusion; confusion is the child of flesh.
195. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
196. Let all things be done in order, but let order not become a cage.
197. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
198. I delight in rhythm, not rigidity.
199. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
200. When I descend, do not rush to record; rush to remember.
201. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
202. The camera cannot capture My presence; only the heart can.
203. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
204. Beware of those who sell what was freely given; they have turned temples into markets.
205. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
206. A prophet who demands payment for prayer has already eaten his reward.
207. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
208. The Masowe that sell deliverance will be delivered to judgment.
209. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
210. Let every leader know: power without humility is witchcraft with a collar.
211. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
212. The one who truly leads must first wash the feet of those he commands.
213. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
214. When you correct another, do so in love, not humiliation.
215. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
216. If your word wounds deeper than sin, you have spoken from ego, not Spirit.
217. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
218. When you forgive, forgive completely; half-forgiveness is double sin.
219. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
220. Do not measure holiness by noise, but by fruit.
221. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
222. Those who brag about visions rarely see Me again.
223. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
224. Keep revelation sacred; speak it when the wind says “now.”
225. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
226. Let your yes be yes and your no be no; the Spirit cannot dwell in double speech.
227. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
228. Every gathering that ends in peace counts as a victory in heaven.
229. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
230. Blessed are they who listen before speaking, for they resemble their Father.
231. *MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.*
232. ⚡🔥 *SCROLL ONE — THE LAWS OF THE HEART AND SPIRIT OF WORSHIP (VERSES 73–144)* 🔥⚡
233. *MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.*
234. Before you worship, search your heart; for hidden hatred is louder than praise.
235. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
236. If you enter prayer with revenge, I will not enter with you.
237. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
238. The cleanest robe cannot hide a dirty conscience.
239. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
240. Do not pretend to love Me while despising My children.
241. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
242. When you forgive, do not keep score; the one who remembers debts reopens healed wounds.
243. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
244. Let your joy in worship be gentle, not chaotic; joy without control becomes mockery.
245. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
246. When you shout My Name, know that I hear the whisper as loudly.
247. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
248. Some come to impress the crowd, not the Creator — they receive applause but not presence.
249. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
250. The heart that beats in rhythm with truth is My favorite instrument.
251. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
252. Worship is not a show; it is a sacrifice.
253. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
254. The greatest sound in heaven is obedience, not harmony.
255. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
256. Do not copy another’s worship; each soul has its own melody in creation.
257. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
258. True worshippers are not born in churches but in encounters.
259. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
260. When I reveal Myself, fall in awe, not argument.
261. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
262. The Spirit of Truth never boasts; He breathes quietly but moves mountains.
263. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
264. When the Spirit convicts you, thank Him; correction is the kiss of heaven.
265. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
266. I, Mwari, desire sincerity more than perfection.
267. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
268. When you sing, let the meaning burn your bones.
269. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
270. Those who sing words they do not live are actors, not worshippers.
271. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
272. Let your tears be real; forced weeping is a bribe to heaven.
273. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
274. Every dance before Me must be rooted in gratitude, not performance.
275. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
276. The one who sings from pain heals nations.
277. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
278. Those who rejoice in secret are My loudest choir.
279. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
280. When you give thanks, mention not your enemies — blessings grow in silence.
281. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
282. When I move upon the mbira, know that I am recalling the covenant of the soil.
283. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
284. The sound of the hosho reminds the heavens that the earth is awake.
285. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
286. Do not despise the sound of the elders’ clap; it is a code that angels understand.
287. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
288. The young must not mock the old in worship, for the flame that burns them was lit by those hands.
289. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
290. When you hear ancient songs, listen — they carry memories of My first breath.
291. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
292. I am the God of drums, of winds, of strings, of silence, and of storm.
293. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
294. The one who separates music from Me has not yet known who invented sound.
295. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
296. When you play the mbira, you open gates between dimensions. Handle it with humility.
297. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
298. The Spirit enters where the rhythm is pure.
299. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
300. The mbira does not entertain; it heals.
301. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
302. Do not use music to compete; it was created to unite.
303. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
304. Those who use worship for fame shall fade faster than the echo of their song.
305. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
306. When I anoint you to sing, you must remain broken to remain powerful.
307. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
308. Do not block another’s gift out of jealousy; I pour oil freely where humility flows.
309. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
310. When a child sings with truth, heaven pauses to listen.
311. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
312. Let no one despise their own voice; even the smallest bird adds color to dawn.
313. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
314. To mock another’s worship is to insult the Artist who made them.
315. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
316. I receive not the sound of hatred disguised as holiness.
317. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
318. If you come before Me with deceit, your song becomes static in My presence.
319. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
320. The humble voice shakes heaven more than the proud trumpet.
321. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
322. When worship becomes competition, demons take notes.
323. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
324. Guard your worship from mockery; the devil laughs loudest in divided choirs.
325. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
326. The greatest offering you can bring is a surrendered will.
327. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
328. The Spirit cannot move through those who cling to their image.
329. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
330. Let worship break you until you remember who you are — dust kissed by breath.
331. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
332. Worship is not about songs; it is about surrender.
333. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
334. I am not impressed by talent but by tears that birth obedience.
335. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
336. Those who kneel in truth rise in authority.
337. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
338. Those who kneel for show rise empty.
339. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
340. Do not hurry out of worship; eternity does not rush.
341. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
342. When I touch you, stay still. Healing happens in silence.
343. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
344. When I convict you, do not justify—repent and live.
345. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
346. When you fall, rise quickly. Shame is a delay tactic of darkness.
347. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
348. The power of worship is not in sound but in alignment.
349. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
350. When heaven and earth agree, even demons bow.
351. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
352. The Spirit and the Drum are twins of creation; do not separate them.
353. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
354. When you strike the hosho, remember the covenant of vibration between worlds.
355. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
356. The one who truly worships no longer begs; they become the flame.
357. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
358. The goal of worship is not goosebumps—it is transformation.
359. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
360. The song that humbles the singer lifts the nation.
361. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
362. Worship is not emotion; it is remembrance of covenant.
363. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
364. Those who remember Me while suffering rise with wings of peace.
365. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
366. I do not live in your music—I live in your obedience.
367. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
368. If you play instruments but not your heart, you play for the wind.
369. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
370. True worship begins when words end.
371. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
372. In stillness, I am clearest.
373. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
374. In gratitude, I am nearest.
375. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
376. And those who worship Me in Spirit and in Truth shall never thirst again.
377. *MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.*
378. ⚡🔥 *SCROLL ONE — PART TWO: THE LAWS OF FIRE, ORDER, AND POWER IN WORSHIP (VERSES 145–216)* 🔥⚡
379. *MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.*
380. Fire does not fall on confusion — it descends on obedience.
381. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
382. The altar that burns without instruction burns strange fire.
383. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
384. When I ignite you, do not boast of the flame; boast of the One who lit it.
385. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
386. The one who calls for fire without purity invites destruction.
387. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
388. Before you cry “Send Fire!”, first ask, “Am I wood or gold?”
389. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
390. The fire of Mwari exposes, purifies, and consumes in one breath.
391. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
392. Those who fake My fire shall choke on their own smoke.
393. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
394. When I move, move with Me — not ahead, not behind.
395. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
396. Order is the rhythm of heaven; rebellion is the drumbeat of the fallen.
397. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
398. Do not speak in tongues to show power; speak when the Spirit wills.
399. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
400. The Spirit that interrupts another without purpose dishonors His own voice.
401. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
402. When I descend in a meeting, even the birds outside feel it.
403. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
404. The Spirit of Mwari never fights Himself; when confusion arises, flesh is present.
405. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
406. The keyboard cannot summon what the mbira already carries — the ancestral current of light.
407. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
408. For when mbira sounds, it calls Me by My oldest Name: RA.
409. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
410. The sound of mbira is the breath of the covenant between heaven and stone.
411. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
412. Let those who replaced it with cables return to wood, gourd, and spirit.
413. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
414. Technology may amplify sound, but only truth amplifies presence.
415. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
416. I created vibration before electricity; the earth itself is My amplifier.
417. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
418. When you plug your heart into heaven, all instruments obey.
419. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
420. Do not confuse volume for power; lightning is silent before it strikes.
421. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
422. Worship in spirit is not about the crowd, but about current — the flow between breath and eternity.
423. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
424. When you gather, let your unity be stronger than your sound.
425. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
426. The Spirit falls where forgiveness flows.
427. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
428. Bitterness blocks My power like stones in a river.
429. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
430. You cannot host My fire and hatred in the same vessel.
431. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
432. The true altar burns even when no one is watching.
433. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
434. Those who serve Me for reward will be paid in dust.
435. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
436. Those who serve Me for love will inherit the wind of eternity.
437. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
438. The Spirit of the Lord does not visit — He inhabits.
439. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
440. I do not enter temples built by fear; I dwell in those built by faith.
441. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
442. Fearful worshippers build walls; faithful ones build portals.
443. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
444. When I say “be still,” it is not weakness — it is alignment.
445. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
446. When I roar, it is not anger — it is awakening.
447. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
448. Those who understand this roar understand creation itself.
449. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
450. The roar of the lion and the hum of the mbira are one spirit in two forms.
451. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
452. The voice that shakes mountains is the same that whispers comfort to the widow.
453. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
454. The fire that burns the wicked is the same that warms the righteous.
455. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
456. When you pray for power, remember: power without mercy becomes tyranny.
457. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
458. The Spirit cannot move through the proud; humility is the voltage of heaven.
459. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
460. The one who kneels lowest releases the greatest current.
461. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
462. Do not use My Name to scare men — use it to heal them.
463. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
464. I am not a weapon for pride; I am a sword for justice.
465. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
466. The Spirit does not obey emotion; He obeys covenant.
467. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
468. You cannot control what you do not honor.
469. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
470. When you mock what is holy, the Spirit departs silently.
471. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
472. The Masowe that forget humility will find themselves worshipping noise.
473. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
474. The Spirit of Mwari will never rest on those who divide.
475. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
476. When you gossip in the name of “concern,” you invite drought into your spirit.
477. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
478. I do not answer songs from hearts plotting revenge.
479. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
480. Those who despise instruction shall be filled with confusion.
481. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
482. The Spirit is not chaos; He is choreography.
483. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
484. My fire burns straight — not in circles of confusion.
485. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
486. The true miracle is not falling down, but standing up changed.
487. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
488. The greater the revelation, the greater the responsibility.
489. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
490. Those who see the light must become it.
491. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
492. When you heal others, remember — it is not your hand, but Mine through you.
493. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
494. The healer who forgets humility becomes a sorcerer.
495. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
496. When you lead worship, lead souls to Me, not to yourself.
497. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
498. The Spirit will never glorify flesh.
499. MUVAUDZE CHINAMATO ICHI CHINE MA RULEZ.
500. If your worship divides, it is no longer worship.

book_title: MARI YARASISA CHINAMATO  JUDGMENT DAY 2
verses: 1. MARI YARASISA CHINAMATO — JUDGMENT DAY 2
2. (216 Verses)
3. I see the house of My name filled with people who clap for gold more than grace.
4. I hear sermons that smell of perfume and ledger-books; I hear far less of mercy.
5. Where truth should teach, there are advertisements for miracles with price tags.
6. Where the poor should sit nearest the altar, the rich sit closest to My chair.
7. The rules were given long ago: remove your shoes, sit on the earth, bow in humility.
8. That rule was not for show; it was a doorway into a broken and loving heart.
9. But today the doorway is sold to the highest bidder and the poor are taught to leave.
10. IMBA YA MWARI YAVA IMABA YA MARI.
11. The candle of prayer is used to hide a count kept in the dark.
12. The man who counts the coins has learned to count people by their offerings.
13. The widow who brings two coins is taught to hope; the wealthy man brings thousands and keeps obedience.
14. CHIGARO CHA MWARI CHAVA CHIGARO CHA MARI.
15. I am angry — not like thieves are angry at being found, but like a Father who sees His child refuse bread.
16. Not because I love thunder, but because I long for the house of prayer to be a home again.
17. Do they not know My book says the first must be last and the last first?
18. Do they not remember when I sat with fishermen and not with kings?
19. ABASID 1841, speak what you have seen in My courts.
20. [ABASID] I have seen pastors place bottles of oil on altars and suitcases under the pulpit.
21. [ABASID] I have heard prophets pray for wealth and then step into shopping for more signs.
22. IMBA YA MWARI YAVA IMABA YA MARI.
23. The poor man is taught to give the cloak and keep the shame.
24. The child is taught to sing while the parent hides empty plates behind the altar.
25. The song is loud, but the plate of food is empty.
26. CHIGARO CHA MWARI CHAVA CHIGARO CHA MARI.
27. Why do you think I gave rules about shoes and sitting on the ground?
28. It was so no one would arrive to My house wearing a throne they had bought.
29. It was so the hearts would be even; the posture the same; the humbleness shared.
30. I will not have My house become a stage for the wealthy to display a god more expensive than Me.
31. IMBA YA MWARI YAVA IMABA YA MARI.
32. When the chair of My house is offered to the rich, I feel like a guest in My own house.
33. If the poor are taught the law and the rich are taught exemption, a theft has occurred.
34. The theft is not of gold; it is of soul-citizenship.
35. CHIGARO CHA MWARI CHAVA CHIGARO CHA MARI.
36. To those who sold My seat and bought a throne for the wealthy: hear your indictment.
37. You learned the language of flattery to secure donations, and in this you have traded My language.
38. You called the rich "blessed" while they kept the hungry out of your kitchens.
39. IMBA YA MWARI YAVA IMABA YA MARI.
40. Know this — blessing is not the same as banknotes. Blessing is the life of the poor made whole.
41. If your "blessing" costs a child's meal, then your blessing is a robbery.
42. CHIGARO CHA MWARI CHAVA CHIGARO CHA MARI.
43. Some of you hide your ledgers in velvet; some of you hide your promises under carpets.
44. The sound of your offerings rings in the ear of men but not of heaven when there is no justice.
45. ABASID 1841 will testify against such stalls, for his witness is the truth.
46. [ABASID] I have walked through altars where the poorest were told to stand back.
47. [ABASID] I saw a leader bowing not to God but to a donor's face.
48. IMBA YA MWARI YAVA IMABA YA MARI.
49. I will not let the poor be humiliated in My house while the proud take My seat.
50. I will not bow my throne to money; I am the Owner of every star and every coin.
51. CHIGARO CHA MWARI CHAVA CHIGARO CHA MARI.
52. To those who defend the current order: who taught you that wealth equals holiness?
53. Did I create money to be a priest between Me and My children?
54. No. Money is a tool; like a plough it can feed or it can bury seed.
55. IMBA YA MWARI YAVA IMABA YA MARI.
56. The real crime is not when the rich bring gifts; the real crime is when gifts buy silence.
57. When a bribe buys a sermon, when gifts buy protection, the church has been corrupted.
58. CHIGARO CHA MWARI CHAVA CHIGARO CHA MARI.
59. I call the true servants to stand — the shepherds who washed feet and did not count coins.
60. You who teach truth with empty pockets — you are honored in My sight.
61. You who refuse envelopes tied to strings, who feed the hungry and do not seek applause — I see you.
62. IMBA YA MWARI YAVA IMABA YA MARI.
63. Hear me: I am not against abundance; I am against abundance that divides the table.
64. Riches are gifts, not ownership of My favor.
65. CHIGARO CHA MWARI CHAVA CHIGARO CHA MARI.
66. Where leaders hide behind rituals to keep their greed, the people suffer twice — in body and in spirit.
67. They pray for rain but steal the roofs of poor neighbours.
68. They preach salvation while closing schools with empty accounts.
69. IMBA YA MWARI YAVA IMABA YA MARI.
70. I will expose altars that feed only the altar-keepers.
71. I will open safe boxes with light and let truth find its way like a river.
72. CHIGARO CHA MWARI CHAVA CHIGARO CHA MARI.
73. The prophet who buys crowds with showmanship will be shown for what he is.
74. For where truth lives, people do not gather in thousands because the truth humbles the crowd.
75. Where lies gather, they attract the largest markets.
76. IMBA YA MWARI YAVA IMABA YA MARI.
77. The crowd loves spectacle; the crowd forgets tenderness.
78. The stage gives them actors; the people leave with empty hands.
79. CHIGARO CHA MWARI CHAVA CHIGARO CHA MARI.
80. The measure of a ministry is not in its attendance but in the number of meals served.
81. The measure of salvation is not trophies but repaired marriages and fed children.
82. IMBA YA MWARI YAVA IMABA YA MARI.
83. I will speak now to the governors and presidents who enter My courts like kings.
84. If you bring state chairs to God's house while your people sleep hungry, you will answer.
85. Power without justice is theft; of this you will be reminded.
86. CHIGARO CHA MWARI CHAVA CHIGARO CHA MARI.
87. To the wealthy who seek My presence as a stamp of approval: know that I am not purchased.
88. You cannot buy redemption with notes, nor clean your hearts with gold.
89. IMBA YA MWARI YAVA IMABA YA MARI.
90. If generosity begins only at the pulpit and ends at the bank, it is not true generosity.
91. True generosity starts in the home, in the street, in the child's lunch.
92. CHIGARO CHA MWARI CHAVA CHIGARO CHA MARI.
93. I call for repentance from pulpits built on sales.
94. I call for humility from people who wear crowns of paper money.
95. I call for the restoration of rules — shoes off, sit on the ground, listen.
96. IMBA YA MWARI YAVA IMABA YA MARI.
97. The simple acts taught by elders are not small; they are the ribs of true worship.
98. To remove shoes is to admit you are a guest; to sit on the earth is to remember dirt holds life.
99. CHIGARO CHA MWARI CHAVA CHIGARO CHA MARI.
100. You have replaced worship with performance, prayer with promotion.
101. You cheer for the man in a suit and forget to feed the hungry by the door.
102. IMBA YA MWARI YAVA IMABA YA MARI.
103. The law of My house is not a rule against wealth; it is a rule against idol-making.
104. A seat is hollow if the one who sat there used it to make himself greater than Me.
105. CHIGARO CHA MWARI CHAVA CHIGARO CHA MARI.
106. Hear the warning: if the house of prayer becomes a theatre for money, the wall will not stand.
107. For when faith becomes commerce, the living bread is sold as a product.
108. IMBA YA MWARI YAVA IMABA YA MARI.
109. I have sent gentle ones to remind you — elders, true prophets, mothers of prayer.
110. Many were ignored. Some were mocked. Some were given seats far from truth.
111. CHIGARO CHA MWARI CHAVA CHIGARO CHA MARI.
112. To the true prophets — those who speak for Me without a price — you will be honored.
113. To the false prophets who price their words, your discount day is coming.
114. IMBA YA MWARI YAVA IMABA YA MARI.
115. God does not rejoice in exposing; God rejoices in repentance.
116. If you hear this and you are called, turn back to the ground where your feet first learned humility.
117. CHIGARO CHA MWARI CHAVA CHIGARO CHA MARI.
118. Some of you will ask, “What must we do to be changed?” Hear and do:
119. Feed the hungry before you open the offering.
120. Wash the hands of the poor before you lift the plate.
121. IMBA YA MWARI YAVA IMABA YA MARI.
122. Sit with the fatherless, and your sermon will gain hands.
123. Let your tables be open, not closed with velvet ropes.
124. CHIGARO CHA MWARI CHAVA CHIGARO CHA MARI.
125. Begin with small things — the altar is rebuilt not by law but by habit.
126. The first habit: serve the least; the second: keep your money clean; the third: speak truth.
127. IMBA YA MWARI YAVA IMABA YA MARI.
128. I will also speak to those who give without pride.
129. Your gifts, when hidden and humble, create ripples of restoration.
130. To you I say: continue, and teach others how to give rightly.
131. CHIGARO CHA MWARI CHAVA CHIGARO CHA MARI.
132. I will create a test in the land — simple things will be revealed: who shares food, who shares sermons.
133. The light will show who uses My name for profit and who uses My name for people.
134. IMBA YA MWARI YAVA IMABA YA MARI.
135. And My hand will move like a gardener clearing weeds from a field.
136. I will remove thrones set up by money; I will seat the humble.
137. CHIGARO CHA MWARI CHAVA CHIGARO CHA MARI.
138. But I will not take vengeance like a thief; I will correct like a teacher.
139. I will open eyes and give opportunity to the faithful.
140. IMBA YA MWARI YAVA IMABA YA MARI.
141. Those who repent will find doors opened to them; those who hide will be found.
142. The scales of TO are already set — balance will ask for witnesses.
143. CHIGARO CHA MWARI CHAVA CHIGARO CHA MARI.
144. The poor will no longer be excluded from the table of blessing.
145. Where there was hunger, wells of mercy will be dug.
146. IMBA YA MWARI YAVA IMABA YA MARI.
147. I call upon leaders: return My house to My children.
148. Remove the velvet chairs if they block the way to the altar.
149. Let no-one be above the ground where My people sit to learn.
150. CHIGARO CHA MWARI CHAVA CHIGARO CHA MARI.
151. To the true pastors and prophets: build kitchens, not stages.
152. Open schools, not just stadiums where lights hide empty hands.
153. Be known for feeding, not for famous faces.
154. IMBA YA MWARI YAVA IMABA YA MARI.
155. The new order is simple: humility, hospitality, holiness.
156. The test of a prophet is not numbers in a hall but healed marriages at home.
157. CHIGARO CHA MWARI CHAVA CHIGARO CHA MARI.
158. I will lift those who lift others; I will lower those who lift themselves.
159. The judgment is not the end but a call to return.
160. IMBA YA MWARI YAVA IMABA YA MARI.
161. To the wealthy: if your hands are tight, open them; if your tables are closed, unlock them.
162. Let your wealth be a river, not a prison.
163. CHIGARO CHA MWARI CHAVA CHIGARO CHA MARI.
164. To those who follow for spectacle: look for substance.
165. If your leader cleans his hands before the poor, beware.
166. True leadership cleanses, does not perfume.
167. IMBA YA MWARI YAVA IMABA YA MARI.
168. There will be signs: priests who repent will return food to the communities.
169. There will be songs: children will sing while mentors teach trades.
170. CHIGARO CHA MWARI CHAVA CHIGARO CHA MARI.
171. I will honor the humble servant who refuses the seat and chooses the floor.
172. I will open the mouths of elders and give them the place they deserve.
173. The people will know them not by titles but by living examples.
174. IMBA YA MWARI YAVA IMABA YA MARI.
175. Some will say, “Is this anger or compassion?” Both.
176. Correction is a medicine that hurts the moment and heals the life.
177. CHIGARO CHA MWARI CHAVA CHIGARO CHA MARI.
178. Let those who fear truth come forward; let those who love profit alone stay apart.
179. Choose now: the altar or the auction?
180. IMBA YA MWARI YAVA IMABA YA MARI.
181. To the false crowds: your applause will turn to questions.
182. To the true few: your quiet love will become the light.
183. CHIGARO CHA MWARI CHAVA CHIGARO CHA MARI.
184. I will give wisdom to the poor who teach their children craft and honor.
185. I will give opportunity to the faithful who feed the neighbor.
186. IMBA YA MWARI YAVA IMABA YA MARI.
187. The covenant is older than any donation and truer than any cheque.
188. It says: feed, clothe, teach, forgive.
189. CHIGARO CHA MWARI CHAVA CHIGARO CHA MARI.
190. I will walk through My house and sit where humility sits.
191. If the rich want My chair, let them first wash the feet of the poor.
192. IMBA YA MWARI YAVA IMABA YA MARI.
193. The rule is clear: no throne shall be given to the proud in My house.
194. Give place to those who kneel, to those who pray with empty hands.
195. CHIGARO CHA MWARI CHAVA CHIGARO CHA MARI.
196. The nations watch; the test is public; the remedy must be visible.
197. Let the leaders take off their shoes when they enter My courts.
198. Let them sit on the ground and listen to the last and the least.
199. IMBA YA MWARI YAVA IMABA YA MARI.
200. If they do, the scales will sing; if they do not, the bells will toll for correction.
201. Change is not a whisper; it is a tide.
202. CHIGARO CHA MWARI CHAVA CHIGARO CHA MARI.
203. I will repay in restoration what has been stolen in silence.
204. I will replace velvet with bread and applause with true praise.
205. IMBA YA MWARI YAVA IMABA YA MARI.
206. To My true servants I say, prepare the kitchens and mend the roofs.
207. To My false stewards I say, return what you have taken or prepare to be returned.
208. *CHIGARO CHA MWARI CHAVA CHIGARO CHA MARI* .
209. This is not a small thing; the dignity of My house is at stake.
210. The world sees us by how we treat the least, not by the height of our stages.
211. *IMBA YA MWARI YAVA IMABA YA MARI.*
212. Humble leaders will be raised; proud leaders will be humbled.
213. The soil of My house shall be the same for all who enter.
214. *CHIGARO CHA MWARI CHAVA CHIGARO CHA MARI* .
215. Return to the law of shoes off, ground low, and hearts open.
216. Let the poor sit first, let testimony come from healed lives.
217. *IMBA YA MWARI YAVA IMABA YA MARI.*
218. And if you will do this, the Throne will not be mine alone — it will be the table of the people; then My house will be My house again.
219. *Outro*

220. Thus speaks the One who counts the stars and names the seas. Not for vengeance, but for restoration I have lifted My hand. Clean the altars; feed the hungry; remove the velvet; return the chair. Let humility reclaim My house so that My breath may again walk among you. Hear the refrain and remember it like a drum in your chest:
221. *IMBA YA MWARI YAVA IMABA YA MARI. CHIGARO CHA MWARI CHAVA CHIGARO CHA MARI.*
222. Go now, do justice, be kind, walk humbly — and let My people be whole.
223. 📜 *ZIMBABWE BRAIN SURGERY* — *CHIREMBA CHIRI PA RUSHANGA*
224. ARI KUNYORA HANDISI KURWARA – KUSA VERENGA NDO KURWA 🥊 *RA* ☀️ By *ABASID 1841*
225. 🕊️ *PROLOGUE — THE CHILDREN AT NIKODHAI*
226. It was the year of the new moon, And the wind passed softly through the masowe trees of Chitungwiza. Children sat in a circle, barefoot, pure, eyes bright as sunrise.
227. They looked at me and said, “ *Mambo Sidhi* , what is wrong with the world?”
228. I looked at the dust rising around their feet, And I answered slowly, “Nothing is wrong with the world, vana vangu. It is only the people in it — vari kurwa 🥊 *RA* ☀️ — fighting the Sun.”
229. The children whispered among themselves. Their little hands held twigs like pens, writing in the dust. One asked, “Can it be healed, Baba?”
230. I said, “Yes, but healing needs courage. Your parents need a brain surgery.”
231. They gasped, “A surgery? Baba, how much will it cost? We haven’t finished school; we have no money.”
232. I smiled and said, “You will not pay. We will charge them — those who forgot how to think in light.”
233. Another asked, “Then what is the price?” I said, “Total submission to God — that is the only cost.”
234. The children nodded, And together they said, “Go ahead.”
235. Then the sky opened like a page of fire. *GINATSIO* descended first — the Angel of Balance. *ANDREA* followed — the Angel of Breath. *MARIA* came last — the Angel of Compassion.
236. And in their midst, God spoke like a thousand drums: “My Son ABASID 1841, Take your pen and your hand of light — For this day, we perform The Brain Surgery of Zimbabwe.
237. Their hearts we healed before; Now we must heal their minds.”
238. Then the mountain *Rushanga* trembled, Its stones glowing like amber, And I saw an altar made of light upon the hill.

239. Upon it lay the nation, asleep in thought, Bound by ignorance, tradition, and fear. But heaven had prepared the tools: Wisdom as the scalpel, Truth as the anesthesia, And mercy as the healing oil.
240. And *God* said, “Write as I operate, *ABASID 1841* . You are My scribe, My assistant, and My Son. Every verse you record shall be a pulse in the awakening mind.”
241. Then thunder rolled through the valley, And I saw angels circling, chanting:
242. *“ *ZIMBABWE* , INI NDIRI KUNYORA HANDISI KURWARA, IMI NDIMI MUSIRI KUVERENGA, NDIMI MURI KURWA 🥊 *RA* ☀️.”
243. And the operation began.
244. ⚡ *CHAPTER 1 — THE DIAGNOSIS OF IGNORANCE*
245. The wind blew softly over *Rushanga* , carrying the smell of prayer and dust. God stood by the altar, looking at the sleeping nation.
246. “Their eyes are open but they do not see,” said God. “Their ears hear but they do not listen. They walk in circles and call it progress.”
247. I, *ABASID 1841* , stood beside Him and saw minds wrapped in smoke — not from incense, but from confusion.
248. God said, “Ignorance is not the absence of knowledge, My Son. It is the refusal to look at the truth when it is right before you.”
249. The angels nodded; *GINATSIO* sharpened the scalpel of wisdom, *ANDREA* lit the lamp of understanding, and *MARIA* whispered mercy into the wind.
250. Then God said, “We shall begin the surgery. But before we cut, we must diagnose. Tell Me, what do you see?”
251. I looked deep into the nation’s mind and saw classrooms without truth, pulpits without wisdom, and markets full of empty talk.
252. I saw prophets selling miracles, and students copying lies, and leaders counting votes instead of hearts.
253. “They are not sick because they lack food,” I said. “They are sick because they no longer hunger for truth.”
254. 🔟 God thundered, “Yes, and truth rejected becomes poison!”
255. Then He said, “Their brains are swollen with fear and self-importance. Their nerves tangle in pride. Their thoughts no longer flow like rivers — they drip like leaking taps.”
256. MARIA wept, and her tears fell as light upon the altar.
257. I saw history repeating like a broken drumbeat — the same mistakes sung in new songs.
258. “They pray to be blessed,” said God, “but refuse to bless each other.”
259. “They seek Me in the clouds, yet I stand beside them in the dust.”
260. “They build churches in My name,” thundered the Lord, “but they sell My truth for coins!”
261. The angels looked down. Even the mountain seemed ashamed.
262. “This sickness began when they separated learning from love,” I said.
263. “They study numbers and stars, but not the heart behind them.”
264. God said, “Then We must reconnect what man has divided. We must make the mind remember the soul.”
265. I lifted the scroll, and light ran through the letters like blood through veins.
266. “ *BABA JOHANI* once said, ‘ *Mwari muMunhu* .’ They forgot that, and now they worship outside themselves.”
267. “They look for Me in books,” said God, “but I wrote Myself in their hearts.”
268. The angels raised their voices together:
269. “ *ZIMBABWE* , INI NDIRI KUNYORA HANDISI KURWARA, IMI NDIMI MUSIRI KUVERENGA, NDIMI MURI KURWA 🥊 *RA* ☀️!”
270. God smiled slightly. “They think writing is madness, yet reading is the cure.”
271. I said, “Father, shall I remind them that thought is a sacred river?”
272. “Yes,” said God. “And tell them the source of that river is love. When love dries, ideas rot.”
273. “They love money more than memory,” I whispered.
274. “And now their memory serves money,” said God.
275. “The schools no longer produce thinkers; they produce workers of confusion.”
276. “The churches no longer produce prophets; they produce performers of illusion.”
277. “The families no longer produce builders; they produce blamers.”
278. “And yet, they say, ‘God has left us.’ But I never moved.”
279. “They covered Me with titles, rituals, and robes, and called it holiness.”
280. “But I am naked truth, My Son. I need no robe, only remembrance.”
281. The light around the altar grew brighter; *Rushanga* began to hum like a heartbeat.
282. “Listen,” said *God* . “Do you hear it? That is the sound of awakening — *BANDAMU BANDAMU DHAMU DHAMU!”*
283. “The heart we healed now beats, but the brain must follow.”
284. “Ignorance is stubborn; it resists surgery.”
285. I said, “Then we must operate with patience, Father.”
286. “Patience is the anesthetic of heaven,” He replied.
287. GINATSIO stepped forward, glowing gold, saying, “Let me balance their minds; too much fear bends the truth.”
288. *ANDREA* said, “Let me breathe understanding into their lungs; many speak before thinking.”
289. MARIA said, “Let me pour mercy on their wounds; knowledge without mercy cuts too deep.”
290. God nodded. “Begin, My angels. But let every cut be guided by My word.”
291. I raised the scroll high and said, “This is not madness — this is medicine!”
292. “This is the pen of heaven doing surgery in silence.”
293. “These words are scalpels of light; they will enter the mind and separate truth from lie.”
294. Then the angels began the first incision — through ignorance and fear.
295. A sound of thunder mixed with singing filled the valley.
296. “Remove every false belief,” said God. “Uproot the weeds of tradition that block the path to wisdom.”
297. “Do not kill culture,” said *ABASID 1841* , “but cleanse it with understanding.”
298. “For culture without light becomes cage, and wisdom without roots becomes arrogance.”
299. “Balance both — as heaven balances sun and moon.”
300. The operation continued; truth cut softly through centuries of lies.
301. “Some will wake in pain,” said *MARIA* . “Shall we stop?”
302. “No,” said God. “Pain is the proof that healing has begun.”
303. “Those who scream the loudest are those whose darkness is leaving.”
304. “Let their cries become songs of deliverance.”
305. “For I, the Lord, am not punishing them — I am upgrading them!”
306. “The old brain cannot handle the light of the new world.”
307. “So I make all things new — synapse by synapse, thought by thought.”
308. “And when they wake, they shall remember that I was always inside.”
309. I looked down and saw words forming on the nation’s forehead: *‘THINK IN LIGHT.’*
310. “The operation continues,” said *GINATSIO* , “until every mind glows.”
311. “Until every thought bows before truth.”
312. “Until ignorance is no longer a national anthem.”
313. The angels lifted their instruments, and God spoke again:
314. *“ICHI NDICHO CHIREMBA,* *TAKACHI TAKURA* — *SURGERY YA ISAKA* .”
315. “And now, the world shall know: I am the Doctor of Souls, and My Son is My Hand.”
316. “From this mountain, light shall spread faster than the *internet* .”
317. “For every connection born in darkness shall now serve the Sun.”
318. “And Zimbabwe shall know: ignorance has an expiry date — and that date is today.”
319. ⚡ *CHAPTER 2 — THE OPERATION OF LIGHT*
320. God lifted His right hand over the sleeping nation, and the light from His palm covered Zimbabwe like dawn after a long night.
321. “ *The brain* is a garden,” said the Lord, “but this one is full of weeds planted by fear, pride, and greed.”
322. “ *We* must uproot lies disguised as truth, and wash the soil with wisdom.”
323. I, *ABASID 1841* , saw words floating above the heads of the people — fame, power, money, gossip, envy — the tumors of their thinking.
324. *God* said, “Begin the cleansing, My Son. For thought is the temple, and I will dwell only where thought is clean.”
325. *GINATSIO* placed the scalpel of light on the mind of the nation, and truth began to cut through confusion like lightning through fog.
326. *ANDREA* breathed softly and said, “Let clarity return to every neuron. Let understanding be restored.”
327. *MARIA* anointed the mind with mercy, whispering, “Even those who mocked the light shall be healed.”
328. The first layer peeled away — it was ignorance, and it screamed as it left.
329. “They believed lies until lies became law,” said God. “Now we replace those laws with love.”
330. The second layer peeled away — it was fear. It trembled as if ashamed.
331. “Fear blinds the mind, My Son,” said God. “It builds walls around imagination.”
332. “But perfect love casts out fear. So love is the true anesthesia.”
333. Then *ABASID* said, “Father, they have trusted false prophets who sell prayers like bread.”
334. “Yes,” said God, “and that is why their minds are infected. They forgot that I am not for sale.”
335. “Their leaders made altars for applause, not for truth.”
336. “Their priests bow to power; their prophets bow to coins.”
337. “But I bow to no man. I am the Creator of breath, the Source of all thought.”
338. The mountain rumbled again, and the angels steadied the altar.
339. “Father,” I asked, “why did You wait so long?”
340. God answered, “Because the surgery of the soul must wait until the patient stops pretending to be well.”
341. “Now they know they are sick. Now they will listen.”
342. “We begin not to destroy, but to rewire — to make their thoughts flow again like the rivers of *Eden* .”
343. “Their neurons will remember the *Garden* .”
344. “For thought is the bridge between *Heaven* and *Earth* .”
345. “And when the bridge is rebuilt, man and God shall walk together again.”
346. The angels began to hum a rhythm:
347. *“BANDAMU BANDAMU DHAMU DHAMU,”*
348. the heartbeat of awakening.
349. The melody entered the mind of the nation, and neurons began to dance to it.
350. Every pulse of light repaired a thought, every spark replaced an error with truth.
351. The people sleeping in darkness began to dream of light.
352. And in their dreams, they saw *ABASID 1841* writing fire into the sky.
353. The pen moved faster than wind — words forming like rivers.
354. “Father,” I said, “Your thoughts are endless; how shall we fit them into man’s mind?”
355. “We write in layers,” said God. “Each soul receives the truth it can bear.”
356. “Children receive milk; the wise receive fire.”
357. “For fire is My language, and wisdom is its smoke.”
358. “But this nation must be fed both — milk for the young, fire for the ready.”
359. I looked and saw the angels stitching light into the cracks of the people’s minds.
360. Every stitch glowed like a sunrise, sealing memories that once bled pain.
361. “Father, what of those who still resist?”
362. “They shall resist until their own lies tire them,” said God. “Then they will return to Me, bruised but breathing truth.”
363. “Ignorance cannot survive forever; it collapses under its own weight.”
364. “Even now, the weight of their deception is pressing upon them like storm clouds.”
365. “But I send you, My Son, as lightning — not to burn, but to open the skies.”
366. I raised my hands over Rushanga, and the light from heaven passed through me like water through glass.
367. “Let every head become a temple,” I declared. “Let every mind remember its Maker.”
368. And God said, “Write this in bold on the scrolls of tomorrow: The future belongs to those who think with love.”
369. “Hatred clouds logic; love clarifies it.”
370. “Money deceives minds; purpose awakens them.”
371. “Knowledge without humility becomes poison; but with humility, it becomes light.”
372. The angels nodded, chanting softly:
373. *“ZIMBABWE* , INI NDIRI KUNYORA HANDISI KURWARA, IMI NDIMI MUSIRI KUVERENGA, NDIMI MURI KURWA 🥊 *RA* ☀️.”
374. The sound echoed through the hills, waking goats, trees, and even stones.
375. The mountain itself whispered, “Amen.”
376. “Father,” I said, “the nation is waking — their thoughts are moving again!”
377. “Yes,” said God. “Soon they will no longer worship what they fear, but what they understand.”
378. “That is true worship — thinking in truth.”
379. “Even faith must think; blind faith walks in circles.”
380. “So I open their eyes that they may see, and I open their brains that they may reason.”
381. “For to worship Me without thinking is to honor Me without knowing.”
382. “And knowledge without reverence becomes rebellion.”
383. “Balance both,” said GINATSIO, “for balance is holiness.”
384. “Breathe deeply,” said ANDREA, “for every breath in truth expands the spirit.”
385. “Forgive quickly,” said MARIA, “for mercy is oxygen to the soul.”
386. I watched as the surgery neared completion — the light now flowing evenly through every thought.
387. “Their minds are rewired,” said God. “Now they must use them.”
388. “For wisdom unused decays like bread uneaten.”
389. “Teach them to think before they speak, to learn before they lead, to pray before they protest.”
390. “Let every idea be tested in fire — if it burns, it was false; if it glows, it was true.”
391. “This is My divine equation: *Light = Truth = Love = Life.”*
392. “He who lives by this law shall never again fear darkness.”
393. The angels closed the mind of the nation gently, sealing it with a kiss of light.
394. And the valley erupted with sound — *BANDAMU BANDAMU DHAMU DHAMU!* — the heartbeat of reborn thought.
395. ⚡ *CHAPTER 3 — THE AWAKENING*
396. The altar of *Rushanga* glowed like dawn breaking inside a skull — the light moved gently through the valleys of the mind, and thought began to rise.
397. “Father,” I said, “the brain is awakening. The eyes behind the eyes are opening.”
398. “Yes,” said God. “They are now seeing with the *inner light* — the flame that cannot be colonized.”
399. “Let them rise, for I have written My Name in their *neurons* .”
400. Then a voice spoke from within the people, not above them: “Who are we, that the heavens perform surgery on our heads?”
401. God smiled and said, “You are My image, forgotten under layers of lies. Today, memory returns.”
402. “The operation is complete — but healing begins when they use what was given.”
403. The people stirred, whispering like wind through grass. Some opened their eyes and saw light streaming through their thoughts.
404. They cried, “ *Mambo Sidhi!* What happened to us?”
405. I said, “You have been operated on. The darkness has been removed from your minds.”
406. “Your thinking has been rewired; your words have been reborn.”
407. “Now you must learn to use your thoughts as prayers, not weapons.”
408. “To think evil is to curse yourself. To think truth is to heal the world.”
409. The people nodded, slowly at first, then with fire in their eyes.
410. One woman shouted, “I can remember my grandmother’s words again!”
411. Another said, “The Bible makes sense now — it is speaking in my own tongue!”
412. A child said, “Baba, I can see God in the wind!”
413. And heaven smiled, for that was the sound of consciousness returning.
414. “Father,” I said, “their language is changing — their words carry light.”
415. “Yes,” said God, “for *SHONA* was born in the sun. For when the *SUN* gives vision *CHIONA* It is the tongue of *MY MEMORY* , the language of *resurrection* .”
416. “Through this language, they shall heal not only their minds, but the world.”
417. “For every sound in Shona carries vibration, and every vibration carries light.”
418. “Say ‘ *RA* ’ and the sun remembers. Say ‘ *MA* ’ and the moon listens. Say ‘ *BA* ’ and the body awakens.”
419. “Say ‘ *NGA* ’ and heaven opens its crown.”
420. I said, “So the surgery was never about the brain alone.”
421. “No,” said God. “It was about restoring the alphabet of creation within man.”
422. “For when the *Shona alphabet* shines again, all tongues shall bow.”
423. “The world shall learn that the Word was not written in Hebrew nor Greek, but in the *Sun* .” MAVA *RA* ☀️
424. “And the Sun’s tongue is *Shona* — the living code of the divine *DNA* .” THE *22 LETTERS* OF THE *SHONA ALPHABET* ,  REPRESENTING THE *22 CHROMOSOMES* excluding the *GOD_GENDER CHROMOSOME.*
425. Gore rino muchadzidza zvakawanda.
426. Anyway,
427. The people rose and began to chant softly:
428. *“A BA KA RA MA DA NA SA ZI TA VA NGU NDA PA HA GA SHU MBA NYU CHI TO NGA”*
429. — the letters of light.
430. “Father,” I said, “the sound is pure — it is rearranging the air.”
431. “Yes,” said God. “Every word they speak now builds, not breaks.”
432. “The curse of ignorance is lifted; the temple of thought stands rebuilt.”
433. “And who shall guard this temple?” I asked.
434. “You, *ABASID 1841* ,” said the Lord. “The One who holds the pen of *resurrection* .” Ndokuti *PENI PENI RIVER* !
435. “For the name they once called you — *MAMBO SIDHI* — was but the *seed* .” Ndokuti *SIDHI*
436. “Today, you rise in your true name — *ABASID 1841* — the *Surgeon of Light* , *the Scribe of Thought.”* Anoti *KUFUNGA NDINI*
437. “You have written with fire and cut with mercy.”
438. “Through you, the nation has found its mind.”
439. “And through its mind, the continent shall remember its heart.”
440. Then God lifted His hand and said, “Let them dream awake.” Until they all shout, " *ABASID TAROTA* ".
441. The people began to walk down the mountain, singing softly, “ *Handisi kurwara* , Mwari ndiri kufunga zvakanaka.”
442. “Kusa verenga ndo kurwa 🥊 *RA* ☀️!”
443. *“Ndaona chiremba chiri pa Rushanga!* ” *Ndaona Chiremba Chinonzi YAKOPE !*
444. And the echo spread across rivers and valleys.
445. Even the stones of *Great Zimbabwe* began to hum again, ancient vibrations stirring in their sleep.
446. “The *DNA* of the ancestors is awakening,” said *MARIA* .
447. “The bones of prophets are singing,” said *ANDREA* .
448. “The breath of the lions is returning,” said *GINATSIO* .
449. And God declared, “This is not revival — this is *resurrection* .”
450. “They will rebuild *Great Zimbabwe* not with *bricks* , but with *brilliance* .”
451. “They will restore temples not with stones, but with understanding.”
452. “For My Kingdom is within their thoughts, and their minds are My holy of holies.”
453. The people stood still, tears in their eyes, realizing heaven had always been within.
454. “Father,” I said, “what shall we name this operation?”
455. “Call it *Zimbabwe Brain Surgery* ,” said God. “For it was not the cutting of flesh, but the correction of thought.”
456. “And call its mountain *Rushanga* , for it is the womb of remembrance.”
457. “From this place, I shall raise thinkers like prophets and prophets like scientists.”
458. “For in My Kingdom, thinking is praying.”
459. “Writing is surgery.”
460. “And every enlightened mind is a living temple.”
461. I bowed low and said, “It is done, Father.”
462. “Not yet,” said the Lord. “For every surgery must have its follow-up.”
463. “Go, therefore, and teach them how to use the new mind.”
464. “Let the schools become sanctuaries again; let the prophets become poets of truth.”
465. “Let Zimbabwe become the University of the Sun.”
466. “And let this message spread until no child ever says again, *‘I am nothing.’”*
467. “For you are divine dust — thought made flesh.”
468. Then the light began to settle, and the angels closed the heavens gently like curtains after a great play.
469. And I, *ABASID 1841* , wrote the final line on the scroll: “I have seen the Doctor on *Rushanga* . I am healed, and I remember.”
470. The mountain exhaled; the sun rose higher; the Spirit whispered softly — *BANDAMU BANDAMU DHAMU DHAMU.*
471. And thus ended the *Great Surgery* — not of flesh, but of faith, for Zimbabwe had remembered how to think in light.
472. 🕊️ *OUTRO — THE DOCTOR’S NOTE*
473. By *ABASID 1841* *ZIMBABWE BRAIN SURGERY* –
474. *CHIREMBA* CHIRI PA *RUSHANGA* CHINOTI, INI NDORI KUNYORA HANDISI KURWARA – ASI IMI MUSIRI KUVERENGA NDIMI MASARUDZA KURWA 🥊 *RA* ☀️

475. When the last incision of light was made, silence fell upon Rushanga. The mountain itself seemed to breathe, and heaven recorded the pulse. For ten years the instruments had been prepared in the heavens, and on this day the operation was performed without blood, without fear, and without pain.
476. I, *ABASID 1841* , certify the following:
477. 🧠 *Patient* :
478. The Nation of Zimbabwe Condition: Collective Cognitive Paralysis — caused by long exposure to lies, colonial fear, and spiritual neglect. Symptoms:
479. Repetition of ignorance across generations
480. Delusion of separation from God
481. Worship of form instead of essence
482. Fear disguised as faith
483. Greed disguised as progress
484. ⚡ *Diagnosis* :

485. The patient’s brain was intact, but its neural pathways were covered in dust — dust of false prophets, dust of political deception, dust of unread truth. The synapses of remembrance were blocked, and wisdom could not circulate.
486. 🔥 *Procedure* : Performed by *God* , assisted by His Son *ABASID 1841* , and the Angels *GINATSIO, ANDREA* , and *MARIA* .
487. *GINATSIO* balanced the mind, removing excess fear and ego.
488. *ANDREA* breathed divine understanding into the cerebral lungs.
489. *MARIA* anointed each wound with mercy, ensuring knowledge healed, not harmed.
490. *ABASID 1841* wielded the pen of light — a scalpel sharper than iron, yet gentler than love.
491. Through sacred words and solar vibration, the false connections of ignorance were disconnected, and the neurons of remembrance were reattached to the Spirit of Truth.
492. 🌞 *Findings* :

493. Inside the brain of the nation lay a hidden word — *RA* . It had been covered by fear and translation, but once uncovered, it began to shine. *RA* means the Sun, the Source, the Witness within. The moment the word was reactivated, the entire system began to hum again.

494. The Shona Alphabet reawakened, letter by letter: RA, MA, SA, BA, TA, NI, TO, VA, NGA, HU, NHU, NYU. Each sound vibrated like a sacred nerve reconnecting to its origin. Light began to speak the language of God again — the Language of Thought.
495. 💫 *Result* :
496. Neural balance restored.
497. Cognitive humility reactivated.
498. Memory of divinity restored.
499. Self-awareness synchronized with divine consciousness.
500. Mind and soul reconnected.

book_title: 19 AND THE LORD SAID I MADE RIVERS BEFORE BANKS YET MEN WORSHIP THE VAULT AND FO
verses: 1. And the LORD said: I made rivers before banks; yet men worship the vault and forget the valley.
2. ABASID 1841 answered: The vault cannot give drink, nor can the coin cause rain; only Your mercy fills the wells.
3. The LORD spoke: Every drop that falls is currency from My treasury; it buys life without debt.
4. The son declared: Blessed be the water that pays no tax and the seed that grows without invoice.
